description: Abdominal aortic aneurysm (AAA) is a localized enlargement of the abdominal aorta such that the diameter is greater than 3 cm or more than 50% larger than normal.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes monitoring small aneurysms and surgical repair for larger, rapidly growing aneurysms. Lifestyle changes such as quitting smoking and managing blood pressure are also important.

description: A hernia (pl.: hernias or herniae, from Latin, meaning 'rupture') is the abnormal exit of tissue or an organ, such as the bowel, through the wall of the cavity in which it normally resides.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgical repair to return the protruding tissue or organ to its proper location. Monitoring is an option for small hernias causing minimal symptoms; lifestyle changes can prevent worsening.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: A peritonsillar abscess (PTA), also known as a quinsy, is an accumulation of pus due to an infection behind the tonsil.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves antibiotics to combat the infection. Drainage of the abscess may be necessary in some cases.

description: Lung abscess is a type of liquefactive necrosis of the lung tissue and formation of cavities (more than 2 cm) containing necrotic debris or fluid caused by microbial infection.
treatment_summary: Treatment usually involves antibiotics to fight the infection. In some cases, drainage of the abscess or surgery may be required.

description: A peritonsillar abscess (PTA), also known as a quinsy, is an accumulation of pus due to an infection behind the tonsil.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically includes antibiotics to treat the infection. Drainage of the abscess is often necessary.

description: Acanthosis nigricans is a cutaneous finding characterized by brown-to-black, velvety hyperpigmentation of the skin, most often affecting body folds such as the posterior neck, axillae, groin and umbilicus.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on addressing the underlying cause, such as managing diabetes or weight loss. Topical treatments can help improve the skin's appearance.

description: Acariasis is a medical condition characterized by infestation of human or animal tissues by mites, a diverse group of microscopic arthropods belonging to the order Acari.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves topical medications to kill the mites. Improving hygiene and sanitation are important preventative measures.

description: Esophageal achalasia, often referred to simply as achalasia, is a failure of smooth muscle fibers to relax, which can cause the lower esophageal sphincter to remain closed.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include medications to relax the esophageal sphincter, endoscopic procedures like pneumatic dilation, or surgery. These aim to improve the passage of food into the stomach.

description: Acne, also known as acne vulgaris, is a long-term skin condition that occurs when dead skin cells and oil from the skin clog hair follicles.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include topical and oral medications to reduce inflammation and oil production. Good skin hygiene and avoiding triggers can also help.

description: Actinic keratosis (AK), sometimes called solar keratosis or senile keratosis, is a pre-cancerous area of thick, scaly, or crusty skin.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves removing or destroying the affected skin cells. Options include topical medications, cryotherapy (freezing), or surgical removal.

description: Bronchiolitis is inflammation of the small airways also known as the bronchioles in the lungs.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and ensuring adequate oxygenation. Supportive care includes hydration and monitoring breathing.

description: Acute bronchitis, also known as a chest cold, is short-term bronchitis – inflammation of the bronchi (large and medium-sized airways) of the lungs.
treatment_summary: Treatment mainly involves relieving symptoms such as cough and chest discomfort.  Cough suppressants or bronchodilators may be prescribed.

description: Beta blockers, also spelled β-blockers and also sometimes known as β-adrenergic receptor antagonists, are a class of medications predominantly used to manage abnormal heart rhythms (arrhythmia) and to protect the heart from a second heart attack after the first one (secondary prevention).
treatment_summary: Bronchodilators are used to relax airway muscles and improve breathing.  Inhaled corticosteroids may be used to reduce inflammation.

description: Acute fatty liver of pregnancy is a rare life-threatening complication of pregnancy that occurs in the third trimester or the immediate period after delivery.
treatment_summary: Prompt delivery of the baby is the primary treatment to prevent maternal and fetal complications. Supportive care focuses on managing complications such as bleeding and liver failure.

description: Glaucoma is a group of eye diseases that can lead to damage of the optic nerve, which transmits visual information from the eye to the brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to lower intraocular pressure quickly to prevent optic nerve damage. Eye drops, oral medications, or laser surgery may be used.

related_diseases.disease: central retinal artery or vein occlusion
retinopathy due to high blood pressure

description: Acute kidney injury (AKI), previously called acute renal failure (ARF), is a sudden decrease in kidney function that develops within seven days, as shown by an increase in serum creatinine or a decrease in urine output, or both.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on addressing the underlying cause and supporting kidney function.  This may include fluid management, medications, or dialysis in severe cases.

description: Otitis media is a group of inflammatory diseases of the middle ear.
treatment_summary: Treatment may involve antibiotics to clear the infection and pain relievers to manage discomfort.  In some cases, watchful waiting is appropriate.

description: Acute pancreatitis (AP) is a sudden inflammation of the pancreas.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on relieving pain, providing intravenous fluids, and nutritional support. Surgery may be needed if there are complications, such as infection or pseudocyst.

description: Acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) is a type of respiratory failure characterized by rapid onset of widespread inflammation in the lungs.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves supportive care to maintain oxygenation and ventilation. Addressing the underlying cause is crucial for recovery.

description: Sinusitis, also known as rhinosinusitis and commonly known as a sinus infection, is an inflammation of the mucous membranes that line the sinuses resulting in symptoms that may include production of thick nasal mucus, nasal congestion, facial congestion, facial pain, facial pressure, loss of smell, or fever.
treatment_summary: Treatment may include nasal decongestants, pain relievers, and antibiotics if a bacterial infection is suspected.  Saline nasal rinses and warm compresses can also provide relief.

description: Acute stress reaction (ASR), also known as psychological shock, mental shock, or simply shock, as well as acute stress disorder (ASD), is a psychological response to a terrifying, traumatic, or surprising experience.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing anxiety and helping the individual process the traumatic event. Medication and therapy are common approaches.

description: Adhesive capsulitis, also known as frozen shoulder, is a condition associated with shoulder pain and stiffness.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce pain and restore range of motion in the shoulder. Physical therapy, pain relievers, and supportive care are typical.

description: Adjustment disorder is a mental disorder defined by a maladaptive response to a psychosocial stressor.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on helping individuals adapt to stressors and manage their emotional response. Therapy and medication may be used.

description: An adrenocortical adenoma or adrenal adenoma is commonly described as a benign neoplasm emerging from the cells that comprise the adrenal cortex.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on hormone production and size of the adenoma. Surgery may be needed for large or hormone-secreting adenomas; otherwise, monitoring is typical.

description: An adrenal tumor or adrenal mass is any benign or malignant neoplasms of the adrenal gland, several of which are notable for their ability to overproduce endocrine hormones.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves surgical removal of the tumor when possible, followed by medication to prevent recurrence. Chemotherapy or radiation may be used.

description: Alcohol abuse encompasses a spectrum of alcohol-related substance abuse.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on behavioral therapy, medication to reduce cravings, and lifestyle changes to support sobriety. Support groups are also helpful.

description: Alcohol intoxication, commonly described in higher doses as drunkenness or inebriation, and known in overdose as alcohol poisoning, is the behavior and physical effects caused by recent consumption of alcohol.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on supportive care, such as monitoring vital signs and preventing complications. Severe cases may require hospitalization.

description: Alcohol withdrawal syndrome (AWS) is a set of symptoms that can occur following a reduction in or cessation of alcohol use after a period of excessive use.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves medications to manage withdrawal symptoms, supportive care, and monitoring for complications. Hospitalization may be required.

description: Alcoholic liver disease (ALD), also called alcohol-related liver disease (ARLD), is a term that encompasses the liver manifestations of alcohol overconsumption, including fatty liver, alcoholic hepatitis, and chronic hepatitis with liver fibrosis or cirrhosis.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on alcohol abstinence, medication to manage liver damage, and supportive care. Liver transplantation may be needed in severe cases.

description: An allergy is an exaggerated immune response where the body mistakenly identifies an ordinarily harmless allergen as a threat.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on avoiding allergens and using medications to relieve symptoms. Immunotherapy may be used for some allergies.

related_diseases.disease: envenomation from spider or animal bite

description: In medicine, animal allergy is hypersensitivity to certain substances produced by animals, such as the proteins in animal hair and saliva.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes antihistamines and topical creams to reduce itching and inflammation. Avoiding contact with the triggering animal is also crucial.

description: Hair loss, also known as alopecia or baldness, refers to a loss of hair from part of the head or body.
treatment_summary: Treatments may include topical medications to stimulate hair growth or oral medications to address underlying conditions. Light therapy can also be used.

description: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a neurodegenerative disease and is the most common form of dementia, accounting for around 60–70% of cases.
treatment_summary: Medications can help manage symptoms, but there is no cure. Supportive care and behavioral therapies can improve quality of life.

description: Amblyopia, also called lazy eye, is a disorder of sight in which the brain fails to fully process input from one eye and over time favors the other eye.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves correcting the underlying cause and forcing the weaker eye to work harder. This is often done with eye patches, glasses, or surgery.

related_diseases.disease: retinopathy due to high blood pressure
central retinal artery or vein occlusion

description: Amyloidosis is a group of diseases in which abnormal proteins, known as amyloid fibrils, build up in tissue.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and reducing amyloid protein production. Organ transplant may be necessary in severe cases.

description: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), also known as motor neuron disease (MND) or Lou Gehrig's disease, is a rare, terminal neurodegenerative disease defined by the progressive loss of both upper and lower motor neurons that normally control voluntary muscle contraction.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to slow disease progression and manage symptoms. Supportive care and palliative care are essential to maintain quality of life.

description: An anal fissure is a break or tear in the skin of the anal canal.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves relieving constipation and reducing pain. Topical creams and, in severe cases, surgery can be used.

description: Anal fistula is a chronic abnormal communication between the anal canal and the perianal skin.
treatment_summary: Surgery is typically required to close the fistula. Antibiotics may be used to treat infection.

description: Anemia (also spelt anaemia in British English) is a blood disorder in which the blood has a reduced ability to carry oxygen.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the cause and may involve iron supplements, dietary changes, or addressing the underlying condition. Blood transfusions may be necessary in severe cases.

related_diseases.disease: anemia due to chronic kidney disease

disease: anemia due to chronic kidney disease
description: Anemia of chronic disease (ACD) or anemia of chronic inflammation is a form of anemia seen in chronic infection, chronic immune activation, and malignancy.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes iron supplements and erythropoiesis-stimulating agents. Management of underlying kidney disease is essential.

description: Anemia of chronic disease (ACD) or anemia of chronic inflammation is a form of anemia seen in chronic infection, chronic immune activation, and malignancy.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing the underlying malignancy and providing supportive care. Oral medications such as iron supplements or erythropoiesis-stimulating agents may be used.

related_diseases.disease: anemia due to chronic kidney disease

description: Anemia of chronic disease (ACD) or anemia of chronic inflammation is a form of anemia seen in chronic infection, chronic immune activation, and malignancy.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing the underlying chronic condition and providing supportive care. Oral medications such as iron supplements or erythropoiesis-stimulating agents may be used.

related_diseases.disease: syndrome of inappropriate secretion of adh (siadh)

description: Angina, in full angina pectoris, is chest pain or pressure, usually caused by insufficient blood flow to the heart muscle (myocardium).
treatment_summary: Treatment includes lifestyle changes, medications to improve blood flow, and in some cases, surgical interventions. Medications may include nitrates, beta-blockers, and calcium channel blockers.

description: Ankylosing spondylitis (AS) is a type of arthritis from the disease spectrum of axial spondyloarthritis.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on pain management and maintaining mobility through physical therapy and medications. NSAIDs and biologics are commonly used.

description: Anxiety is an emotion characterised by an unpleasant state of inner turmoil and includes feelings of dread over anticipated events.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves a combination of behavioral therapy, medication, and lifestyle changes. Common medications include antidepressants and anti-anxiety drugs.

description: Aortic stenosis (AS or AoS) is the narrowing  of the exit of the left ventricle of the heart (where the aorta begins), such that problems result.
treatment_summary: Treatment options range from monitoring mild cases and managing symptoms with medication to surgical valve repair or replacement for severe cases. Medications may include diuretics and ACE inhibitors.

description: Aphakia is the absence of the lens of the eye, due to surgical removal, such as in cataract surgery, a perforating wound or ulcer, or congenital anomaly.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves surgical correction with an intraocular lens implant. Topical medications can manage related inflammation.

related_diseases.disease: retinopathy due to high blood pressure

description: Aphthous stomatitis, or recurrent aphthous stomatitis (RAS), commonly referred to as a canker sore or salt blister, is a common condition characterized by the repeated formation of benign and non-contagious mouth ulcers (aphthae) in otherwise healthy individuals.
treatment_summary: Treatment is mainly supportive, focusing on pain relief with topical treatments and promoting healing. Topical corticosteroids or antimicrobial rinses may be prescribed.

description: Aplastic anemia (AA) is a severe hematologic condition in which the body fails to make blood cells in sufficient numbers.
treatment_summary: Treatment may include supportive care with blood transfusions, medications to stimulate blood cell production, or bone marrow transplantation. Immunosuppressants are sometimes used.

related_diseases.disease: anemia due to chronic kidney disease

description: Appendicitis is inflammation of the appendix.
treatment_summary: The primary treatment is surgical removal of the appendix (appendectomy). Antibiotics are administered to treat infection.

description: Arrhythmias, also known as cardiac arrhythmias, are irregularities in the heartbeat, including when it is too fast or too slow.
treatment_summary: Treatments for arrhythmias include medications to control heart rate and rhythm, surgical procedures such as pacemaker implantation, and lifestyle changes.

description: Acute septic arthritis, infectious arthritis, suppurative arthritis, pyogenic arthritis, osteomyelitis, or joint infection is the invasion of a joint by an infectious agent resulting in joint inflammation.
treatment_summary: Treatment for arthritis of the hip includes pain medication, physical therapy to improve mobility, and surgery such as hip replacement in severe cases.

description: Ascending cholangitis, also known as acute cholangitis or simply cholangitis, is inflammation of the bile duct, usually caused by bacteria ascending from its junction with the duodenum (first part of the small intestine).
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves antibiotics to combat infection, supportive care, and potentially surgical intervention to relieve the obstruction in the bile duct.

description: Asperger syndrome (AS), also known as Asperger's syndrome or Asperger's, is a formerly used diagnostic category for a condition characterized by significant difficulties in social interaction and nonverbal communication, along with restricted and repetitive patterns of behavior and interests.
treatment_summary: Treatment for Asperger syndrome focuses on behavioral therapies to improve social and communication skills, physical therapy, and medication to manage co-occurring conditions.

description: Aspergillosis is a fungal infection of usually the lungs, caused by the genus Aspergillus, a common mold that is breathed in frequently from the air, but does not usually affect most people.
treatment_summary: Aspergillosis is treated with antifungal medications. Supportive care is also important, especially for those with weakened immune systems.

description: Asthma is a common long-term inflammatory disease of the airways.
treatment_summary: Asthma is managed with inhaled corticosteroids and bronchodilators to control inflammation and open airways, along with lifestyle changes to avoid triggers.

description: Astigmatism is a type of refractive error due to rotational asymmetry in the eye's refractive power.
treatment_summary: Astigmatism is corrected with eyeglasses or contact lenses to reshape light entering the eye; refractive surgery is also an option. Eye drops can help with dry eye.

related_diseases.disease: retinopathy due to high blood pressure

description: Atelectasis is the partial collapse or closure of a lung resulting in reduced or absent gas exchange.
treatment_summary: Treatment may include chest physiotherapy to help clear secretions, treating underlying causes, or surgery to remove obstructions. Supportive care such as oxygen therapy may be needed.

description: Athlete's foot, known medically as tinea pedis, is a common skin infection of the feet caused by a fungus.
treatment_summary: Athlete's foot is typically treated with topical antifungal creams or, in more severe cases, oral medications. Keeping feet clean and dry is also essential.

description: Urinary retention is an inability to completely empty the bladder.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves managing urinary retention through catheterization, medications to improve bladder function, and addressing the underlying cause.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: Atrial fibrillation (AF, AFib or A-fib) is an abnormal heart rhythm (arrhythmia) characterized by rapid and irregular beating of the atrial chambers of the heart.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on controlling heart rate and rhythm and preventing blood clots. Medications or surgical procedures may be used.

description: Atrial flutter (AFL) is a common abnormal heart rhythm that starts in the atrial chambers of the heart.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to control the heart rate and rhythm, often with medication or catheter ablation. Preventing blood clots is also important.

description: Steroid-induced skin atrophy is thinning of the skin at the level of the epidermis as a result of prolonged exposure to topical steroids.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves discontinuing or reducing steroid use and using emollients to improve skin hydration. In some cases, laser therapy or other treatments may be considered.

description: Atrophic vaginitis is inflammation of the vagina as a result of tissue thinning due to low estrogen levels.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves hormone replacement therapy or topical estrogen creams to restore vaginal lubrication and elasticity. Lifestyle changes may provide additional relief.

description: The development of the reproductive system is the part of embryonic growth that results in the sex organs and contributes to sexual differentiation.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include medications to improve blood flow or surgical procedures to address underlying causes. Lifestyle modifications are often recommended.

description: Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterised by symptoms of inattention, hyperactivity, impulsivity, and emotional dysregulation that are excessive and pervasive, impairing in multiple contexts, and developmentally inappropriate.
treatment_summary: ADHD is typically treated with a combination of stimulant or non-stimulant medications and behavioral therapy. Lifestyle adjustments, such as structured routines, can also help manage symptoms.

description: Autism, also known as autism spectrum disorder (ASD), is a condition characterized by differences or difficulties in social communication and interaction, a need or strong preference for predictability and routine, sensory processing differences, focused interests, or repetitive behaviors.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on therapies to improve social, communication, and behavioral skills. Medications may be used to manage associated conditions like anxiety or hyperactivity; the goal is supportive care and improved quality of life.

description: The autonomic nervous system (ANS), sometimes called the visceral nervous system and formerly the vegetative nervous system, is a division of the nervous system that operates internal organs, smooth muscle and glands.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing individual symptoms and addressing underlying causes. Lifestyle modifications and medications can help regulate blood pressure, heart rate, and digestion.

description: Avascular necrosis (AVN), also called osteonecrosis or bone infarction, is death of bone tissue due to interruption of the blood supply.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include pain medication, physical therapy, and surgery to improve blood flow or replace the affected joint. Early diagnosis and treatment can slow progression.

description: Balanitis is inflammation of the glans penis.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves maintaining good hygiene and using topical antifungal or steroid creams to reduce inflammation and infection. Circumcision may be considered in recurrent cases.

description: Bell's palsy is a type of facial paralysis that results in a temporary inability to control the facial muscles on the affected side of the face.
treatment_summary: Corticosteroids and antiviral medications may be prescribed. Physical therapy can help to maintain muscle tone.

description: A renal cyst  is a fluid collection in or on the kidney.
treatment_summary: Small, asymptomatic cysts often require only monitoring. Larger, symptomatic cysts may need to be drained or surgically removed.

description: Benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV) is a disorder arising from a problem in the inner ear.
treatment_summary: The Epley maneuver, a type of physical therapy, is highly effective. The treatment repositions the crystals in the inner ear.

description: Benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), also called prostate enlargement, is a noncancerous increase in size of the prostate gland.
treatment_summary: Medications can help relax the prostate or shrink it. Surgery may be necessary for severe cases; lifestyle changes can help manage symptoms.

description: Boric acid is an antiseptic used as a vaginal medication to treat vaginal infections including yeast infections, bacterial vaginosis, and trichomoniasis.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on addressing any underlying infection. Good hygiene practices can help prevent irritation.

description: Bipolar disorder (BD), previously known as manic depression, is a mental disorder characterized by periods of depression and abnormally elevated mood, lasting days to weeks, and in some cases months.
treatment_summary: Mood stabilizers and antipsychotics are commonly prescribed. Psychotherapy helps individuals manage their symptoms and improve coping skills.

description:
Birth trauma may refer to: 

Childbirth-related post-traumatic stress disorder, psychological trauma to the mother following childbirth
Birth trauma (physical), physical trauma to the infant following childbirth, as described at ICD-10 codes P10-P15
Birth trauma (psychoanalysis), a concept in Freudian psychoanalysis described by Otto Rank
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the type and severity of the trauma, potentially including physical and psychological therapies. Supportive care is essential.

description: Bladder cancer is the abnormal growth of cells in the bladder.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include surgery, chemotherapy, and radiation therapy. Immunotherapy may also be an option depending on the stage and type of bladder cancer.

description: Urinary bladder disease includes urinary bladder inflammation such as cystitis, bladder rupture and bladder obstruction (tamponade).
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the specific disorder and may include antibiotics for infections. Surgery may be required for structural problems.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: Bladder outlet obstruction (or obstructive uropathy) occurs when urine is unable to flow from the kidneys through the ureters and out of the bladder through the urethra.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to relieve the obstruction, often through surgery or medication. Catheterization may be needed for temporary relief.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: Blepharitis, sometimes known as granulated eyelids, is one of the most common ocular conditions characterized by inflammation, scaling, reddening, and crusting of the eyelid.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms with warm compresses and eyelid scrubs. Topical antibiotics or steroids may be prescribed in some cases.

description: Blepharospasm is a neurological disorder characterized by intermittent, involuntary spasms and contractions of the orbicularis oculi (eyelid) muscles around both eyes.
treatment_summary: Botulinum toxin injections are a common treatment to paralyze the eyelid muscles. Oral medications can help, and surgery is sometimes considered.

description: A bone tumor is an abnormal growth of tissue in bone, traditionally classified as noncancerous (benign) or cancerous (malignant).
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery to remove the tumor, followed by chemotherapy or radiation therapy. Supportive care addresses pain and other symptoms.

related_diseases.disease: anemia due to chronic kidney disease

description: Roy Lee "Rocky" Dennis (December 4, 1961 – October 4, 1978) was an American teenager who had craniodiaphyseal dysplasia, an extremely rare sclerotic bone disorder.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies greatly depending on the specific bone disorder. It may include medications, physical therapy, or supportive care to manage symptoms.

description: A calcaneal spur, or commonly known as a heel spur, occurs when a bony outgrowth forms on the heel bone.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include physical therapy, pain relievers, and orthotics. Surgery is considered if conservative treatments are ineffective.

description: Parsonage–Turner syndrome (abbreviated PTS), also known as acute brachial neuropathy and neuralgic amyotrophy, is a syndrome of unknown cause; although many specific risk factors have been identified (such as; post-operative, post-infectious, post-traumatic or post-vaccination).
treatment_summary: Pain management with medication and physical therapy to improve strength and range of motion are common treatments. Supportive care focuses on comfort and function.

description: A brain tumor (sometimes referred to as brain cancer) occurs when a group of cells within the brain grow out of control, creating a mass.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery, radiation therapy, and chemotherapy. Palliative care focuses on managing symptoms and improving quality of life.

description: Breast cancer is a cancer that develops from breast tissue.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include surgery, radiation therapy, chemotherapy, hormone therapy, and targeted therapy. Treatment is tailored to the individual and the stage of the cancer.

description: A breast cyst is a cyst, a fluid-filled sac, within the breast.
treatment_summary: Many breast cysts require no treatment and are monitored. If painful or large, aspiration may be performed to drain the fluid.

description: Mastitis is inflammation of the breast or udder, usually associated with breastfeeding.
treatment_summary: Antibiotics are typically prescribed to treat the infection. Supportive care includes pain relief and continued breastfeeding or pumping to empty the breast.

description: Dental trauma refers to trauma (injury) to the teeth and/or periodontium (gums, periodontal ligament, alveolar bone), and nearby soft tissues such as the lips, tongue, etc.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes dental restoration, root canal, or extraction depending on severity. Crowns or fillings can restore tooth function.

description: A bunion, also known as hallux valgus, is an outward deformity of the foot's metatarsophalangeal joint which connects the big toe to the foot.
treatment_summary: Treatment ranges from wider footwear and orthotics to surgical correction. Surgery aims to realign the bone and relieve pain.

description: A burn is an injury to skin, or other tissues, caused by heat, electricity, chemicals, friction, or ionizing radiation (such as sunburn, caused by ultraviolet radiation).
treatment_summary: Treatment varies based on severity, ranging from topical ointments and fluid replacement to skin grafts. Pain management and infection control are also important.

description: Bursitis is the inflammation of one or more bursae in the body.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes rest, ice, and pain relievers. Physical therapy may be recommended to improve range of motion.

description: A callus (pl.: calluses) is an area of thickened and sometimes hardened skin that forms as a response to repeated friction, pressure, or other irritation.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves removing the source of friction or pressure, using padding, and possibly topical medications. In some cases, a podiatrist may need to trim the callus.

description: Carbon monoxide poisoning typically occurs from breathing in carbon monoxide (CO) at excessive levels.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves administering 100% oxygen, often in a hyperbaric chamber. Supportive care addresses any complications.

description: Carcinoid syndrome is a paraneoplastic syndrome comprising the signs and symptoms that occur secondary to neuroendocrine tumors (formerly known as carcinoid tumors).
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms with medications and addressing the underlying tumor. Surgery, chemotherapy, or targeted therapies may be used to treat the tumor.

description: Cardiac arrest, also known as sudden cardiac arrest (SCA), is a condition in which the heart suddenly and unexpectedly stops beating.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves immediate CPR and defibrillation to restore heart rhythm. Advanced care includes medications and potentially implantable devices.

description: Cardiomyopathy is a group of primary diseases of the heart muscle.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to manage symptoms, prevent complications, and sometimes address the underlying cause. Medications, lifestyle changes, and implantable devices may be used.

description: Carpal tunnel syndrome (CTS) is a nerve compression syndrome caused when the median nerve, in the carpal tunnel of the wrist, becomes compressed.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes wrist splints, pain relievers, and physical therapy. Surgery may be needed to relieve pressure on the median nerve.

description: Cat-scratch disease (CSD) is an infectious disease that most often results from a scratch or bite of a cat.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves supportive care to manage symptoms. Antibiotics may be prescribed in more severe or persistent cases.

description: A cataract is a cloudy area in the lens of the eye that leads to a decrease in vision of the eye.
treatment_summary: Cataracts are usually treated with surgery to replace the cloudy lens with an artificial lens, restoring vision.

description: Coeliac disease (Commonwealth English) or celiac disease (American English) is a chronic autoimmune disease, mainly affecting the small intestine.

risk_factors.label: Presence of certain genes (HLA-DQ2 or HLA-DQ8)

treatment_summary: The primary treatment for celiac disease is a strict gluten-free diet. This allows the small intestine to heal and reduces symptoms.

description: Cellulitis is usually a bacterial infection involving the inner layers of the skin.
treatment_summary: Cellulitis and abscesses are typically treated with oral antibiotics to combat the bacterial infection. Incision and drainage may be necessary for abscesses.

description: Atherosclerosis is a pattern of the disease arteriosclerosis, characterized by development of abnormalities called lesions in walls of arteries.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on lifestyle changes, medications to control risk factors, and sometimes surgical procedures to improve blood flow.

disease: central retinal artery or vein occlusion
description: Central retinal vein occlusion, also CRVO, is when the central retinal vein becomes occluded, usually through thrombosis.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce complications and improve vision through medication, laser treatments, or surgery. Regular monitoring is essential.

related_diseases.disease: retinopathy due to high blood pressure

description: Cerebral edema is excess accumulation of fluid (edema) in the intracellular or extracellular spaces of the brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing swelling in the brain, often with medication or surgery.  Monitoring of intracranial pressure is crucial.

description: Cerebral palsy (CP) is a group of movement disorders that appear in early childhood.
treatment_summary: Treatment is multidisciplinary and focuses on improving motor skills, managing symptoms, and maximizing independence. This may include physical therapy, occupational therapy, medications, and surgery.

description: Cervical cancer is a type of cancer that develops in the cervix or in any layer of the wall of the cervix.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include surgery, radiation therapy, and chemotherapy, depending on the stage of the cancer. Early detection through screening is crucial.

description: Cervical spine disorders are illnesses that affect the cervical spine, which is made up of the upper first seven vertebrae, encasing and shielding the spinal cord.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies depending on the specific disorder and may include physical therapy, pain medication, lifestyle modifications, or surgery in severe cases. The goal is to reduce pain and restore function.

description: Cervicitis is inflammation of the uterine cervix.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves antibiotics to clear the infection. Topical creams may be used to soothe inflammation.

description: A chalazion (; plural chalazia or chalazions) or meibomian cyst is not a cyst but a granuloma in the eyelid that results from a blocked meibomian gland.
treatment_summary: Warm compresses are often used initially. If the chalazion persists, topical medications or surgical removal may be necessary.

description: Chickenpox, also known as varicella (  VARR-iss-EL-ə), is a highly contagious disease caused by varicella zoster virus (VZV), a member of the herpesvirus family.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on relieving symptoms such as itching and fever. Antiviral medications may be used in some cases.

description: Chlamydia, or more specifically a chlamydia infection, is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis.
treatment_summary: Chlamydia is treated with antibiotics. It is important to complete the full course of medication to ensure the infection is cleared.

description: Cholecystitis is inflammation of the gallbladder.
treatment_summary: Treatment may involve antibiotics for infection and pain management. In many cases, surgical removal of the gallbladder is required.

description: Common bile duct stone, also known as choledocholithiasis, is the presence of gallstones in the common bile duct (CBD) (thus choledocho- + lithiasis).
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves removing the gallstones from the common bile duct. This can be done endoscopically or surgically.

description: Cholesteatoma is a destructive and expanding growth consisting of keratinizing squamous epithelium in the middle ear and/or mastoid process.
treatment_summary: Treatment always involves surgical removal of the cholesteatoma. This is necessary to prevent further damage to the ear.

description: Chondromalacia patellae (CMP; from Greek  μαλακία (malakia) 'softening'  χόνδρος (chondros) 'cartilage' and Latin  patella 'kneecap') is an inflammation of the underside of the kneecap and softening of the cartilage.
treatment_summary: Physical therapy to strengthen muscles and improve alignment is common. Pain relievers and lifestyle modifications may also help.

description: Chorioretinitis is an inflammation of the choroid (thin pigmented vascular coat of the eye) and retina of the eye.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the cause and may include anti-inflammatory or anti-infective medications. Supportive care is used to manage symptoms.

related_diseases.disease: retinopathy due to high blood pressure

description: Back pain (Latin: dorsalgia) is pain felt in the back.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves a combination of pain medication, physical therapy, and lifestyle changes. Psychological therapies can also help manage chronic pain.

description: Constipation is a bowel dysfunction that makes bowel movements infrequent or hard to pass.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on dietary changes such as increasing fiber and water intake. Laxatives may be used to help relieve constipation.

description: Glaucoma is a group of eye diseases that can lead to damage of the optic nerve, which transmits visual information from the eye to the brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes eye drops to lower eye pressure and prevent further optic nerve damage. Surgery can improve drainage of fluid from the eye.

description: Chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy (CIDP) is an acquired autoimmune disease of the peripheral nervous system characterized by progressive weakness and impaired sensory function in the legs and arms.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes medications to suppress the immune system and reduce inflammation. Physical therapy is important to maintain strength and function.

description: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is a type of long-term kidney disease, defined by the sustained presence of abnormal kidney function and/or abnormal kidney structure.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes controlling blood pressure and blood sugar, and managing diet. Monitoring kidney function is critical to slow progression.

related_diseases.disease: kidney disease due to longstanding hypertension
anemia due to chronic kidney disease

description: Knee pain is pain in or around the knee.
treatment_summary: Treatment often includes pain relievers and physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee. Weight management and activity modification can help.

description: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) is a type of progressive lung disease characterized by chronic respiratory symptoms and airflow limitation.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes bronchodilators and inhaled corticosteroids to improve airflow and reduce inflammation. Pulmonary rehabilitation and smoking cessation are important.

description: Otitis media is a group of inflammatory diseases of the middle ear.
treatment_summary: Treatment may include antibiotics for infection and ear tubes for drainage. Monitoring hearing is important.

description: Chronic pain is pain that persists or recurs for longer than 3 months.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes pain medications, physical therapy, and psychological support to manage pain. A multidisciplinary approach is often needed.

description: Chronic pancreatitis is a long-standing inflammation of the pancreas that alters the organ's normal structure and functions.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes pain management with medications and lifestyle changes, such as abstaining from alcohol and smoking. Surgery may be needed to remove obstructions or damaged parts of the pancreas.

description: Rheumatic fever (RF) is an inflammatory disease that can involve the heart, joints, skin, and brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antibiotics to eradicate the streptococcal infection. Anti-inflammatory medications are given to reduce inflammation, and monitoring for heart complications is important.

description: Sinusitis, also known as rhinosinusitis and commonly known as a sinus infection, is an inflammation of the mucous membranes that line the sinuses resulting in symptoms that may include production of thick nasal mucus, nasal congestion, facial congestion, facial pain, facial pressure, loss of smell, or fever.
treatment_summary: Treatments focus on reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms. Options include medications like nasal corticosteroids and, in some cases, surgery.

description: An ulcer is a discontinuity or break in a bodily membrane that impedes normal function of the affected organ.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to promote healing and prevent infection. This often involves wound care, pressure relief, and addressing underlying medical conditions.

description: Cirrhosis, also known as liver cirrhosis, hepatic cirrhosis, chronic liver failure, chronic hepatic failure, and end-stage liver disease, is a chronic condition of the liver in which the normal functioning tissue, or parenchyma, is replaced with scar tissue (fibrosis) and regenerative nodules as a result of chronic liver disease.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing complications and slowing progression. Liver transplant may be considered in advanced cases.

description: Coagulopathy (also called a bleeding disorder) is a condition in which the blood's ability to coagulate (form clots) is impaired.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the specific disorder and severity of bleeding. It can include medications to promote clotting or blood transfusions.

description: A cold sore is a type of herpes infection caused by the herpes simplex virus that affects primarily the lip.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on relieving symptoms and shortening the duration of outbreaks. Antiviral creams and oral medications are common options.

description: A colorectal polyp is a polyp (fleshy growth) occurring on the lining of the colon or rectum.
treatment_summary: Treatment usually involves removing the polyp during a colonoscopy. Regular screening colonoscopies are recommended to detect and remove polyps early.

description: Colorectal cancer, also known as bowel cancer, colon cancer, or rectal cancer, is the development of cancer from the colon or rectum (parts of the large intestine).

risk_factors.label: diet high in red and processed meats

treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves surgery to remove the tumor, often followed by chemotherapy or radiation therapy. The specific approach depends on the stage and location of the cancer.

description: The common cold, or the cold, is a viral infectious disease of the upper respiratory tract that primarily affects the respiratory mucosa of the nose, throat, sinuses, and larynx.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on relieving symptoms such as fever, cough, and congestion. Rest and hydration are important.

description: Complex regional pain syndrome (CRPS type 1 and type 2), sometimes referred to by the hyponyms reflex sympathetic dystrophy (RSD) or reflex neurovascular dystrophy (RND) (type 1) or causalgia (type 2), is a rare and severe form of neuroinflammatory and dysautonomic disorder causing chronic pain, neurovascular, and neuropathic symptoms.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to manage pain and improve function. It can include medications, physical therapy, and psychological support.

description: A concussion, also known as a mild traumatic brain injury (mTBI), is a head injury that temporarily affects brain functioning.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on rest and symptom management. Gradual return to activity is recommended under medical supervision.

description: Conduct disorder (CD) is a mental disorder diagnosed in childhood or adolescence that presents itself through a repetitive and persistent pattern of behavior that includes theft, lies, physical violence that may lead to destruction, and reckless breaking of rules, in which the basic rights of others or major age-appropriate norms are violated.

risk_factors.label: Family history of mental health issues

treatment_summary: Treatment includes behavioral therapy to address the underlying issues and medication to manage symptoms. Early intervention is important to improve outcomes.

description: Conductive hearing loss (CHL) is a type of hearing impairment that occurs when sound waves are unable to efficiently travel through the outer ear, tympanic membrane (eardrum), or middle ear structures such as the ossicles.
treatment_summary: Treatment may involve addressing the underlying cause such as removing earwax or treating an infection. Surgery can correct structural problems in the middle ear.

description: A congenital heart defect (CHD), also known as a congenital heart anomaly, congenital cardiovascular malformation, and congenital heart disease, is a defect in the structure of the heart or great vessels that is present at birth.
treatment_summary: Treatment ranges from monitoring mild defects to surgery for severe defects. Medication can help manage symptoms before or after surgery.

description: Septo-optic dysplasia (SOD), known also as de Morsier syndrome, is a rare congenital malformation syndrome that features a combination of the underdevelopment of the optic nerve, pituitary gland dysfunction, and absence of the septum pellucidum (a midline part of the brain).
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and providing supportive care. Specific interventions depend on the malformations present.

description: Conjunctivitis, also known as pink eye, is inflammation of the conjunctiva, the thin and clear layer that covers the white surface of the eye and the inner eyelid.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves topical medications like eye drops to combat infection or inflammation. Supportive care includes cool compresses and avoiding irritants.

description: Allergic conjunctivitis (AC) is inflammation of the conjunctiva (the membrane covering the white part of the eye) due to allergy.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes antihistamine eye drops and oral medications to relieve itching and inflammation. Avoiding allergens can also help prevent recurrences.

description: Conjunctivitis, also known as pink eye, is inflammation of the conjunctiva, the thin and clear layer that covers the white surface of the eye and the inner eyelid.
treatment_summary: Bacterial conjunctivitis is typically treated with antibiotic eye drops or ointments. Practicing good hygiene helps prevent the spread of infection.

description: Conjunctivitis, also known as pink eye, is inflammation of the conjunctiva, the thin and clear layer that covers the white surface of the eye and the inner eyelid.
treatment_summary: Viral conjunctivitis typically resolves on its own with supportive care, such as cool compresses. Artificial tears can help alleviate discomfort.

description: Connective tissue diseases (also termed connective tissue disorders, or collagen vascular diseases), are medical conditions that affect connective tissue.
treatment_summary: Treatment is focused on managing the symptoms and slowing the progression of the disease. This includes medications, physical therapy, and supportive care.

related_diseases.disease: syndrome of inappropriate secretion of adh (siadh)

description: Contact dermatitis is a type of acute or chronic inflammation of the skin caused by exposure to chemical or physical agents.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes topical corticosteroids to reduce inflammation. Avoiding the offending substance is crucial for preventing recurrences.

description: Conversion disorder (CD) was a formerly diagnosed psychiatric disorder characterized by abnormal sensory experiences and movement problems during periods of high psychological stress.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on addressing underlying psychological stressors and improving physical functioning. Cognitive behavioral therapy and physical therapy are often employed.

description: The cornea is the transparent front part of the eyeball which covers the iris, pupil, and anterior chamber.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antimicrobial eye drops or oral medications to eliminate the infection. Severe cases may require more intensive intervention.

description: Corneal abrasion is a scratch to the surface of the cornea of the eye.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically includes lubricating eye drops or antibiotic ointment to prevent infection and promote healing. A patch may be used for comfort in some cases.

description: Corneal ectatic disorders or corneal ectasia are a group of uncommon, noninflammatory, eye disorders characterised by bilateral thinning of the central, paracentral, or peripheral cornea.
treatment_summary: Treatment options range from specialized contact lenses and eye drops to corneal transplantation, depending on the severity. The goal is to improve vision and prevent further corneal thinning.

description: Atherosclerosis is a pattern of the disease arteriosclerosis, characterized by development of abnormalities called lesions in walls of arteries.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on lifestyle modifications, medications to lower cholesterol and blood pressure, and surgical procedures like angioplasty or bypass surgery to improve blood flow to the heart.

description: Sixth nerve palsy, or abducens nerve palsy, is a disorder associated with dysfunction of cranial nerve VI (the abducens nerve), which is responsible for causing contraction of the lateral rectus muscle to abduct (i.e., turn out) the eye.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the underlying cause and may involve physical therapy to improve eye movement, surgery to correct misalignment, or management of underlying conditions like diabetes.

description: Crohn's disease is a type of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) that may affect any segment of the gastrointestinal tract.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce inflammation, relieve symptoms, and prevent complications. This may involve medications, lifestyle changes, and in some cases, surgery.

description: Croup ( KROOP), also known as croupy cough, is a type of respiratory infection that is usually caused by a virus.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on relieving symptoms such as cough and difficulty breathing. This may involve corticosteroids to reduce inflammation and supportive care such as humidified air.

description: A crush injury is injury by an object that causes compression of the body.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves stabilizing the patient, managing pain, preventing complications like compartment syndrome and infection, and potentially surgical intervention to repair damaged tissues.

description: Cryptococcosis is a potentially fatal fungal infection of mainly the lungs, presenting as a pneumonia, and in the brain, where it appears as a meningitis.
treatment_summary: Treatment requires antifungal medications, often administered intravenously in severe cases, to eradicate the fungal infection. Supportive care is essential to manage symptoms and potential complications.

related_diseases.disease: syndrome of inappropriate secretion of adh (siadh)

description: Cryptorchidism, also known as undescended testis (UDT), is the failure of one or both testicles to descend into the scrotum.
treatment_summary: Surgery is often performed to bring the testicle into the scrotum. Monitoring may be used for mild cases.

description: Cushing's syndrome is a collection of signs and symptoms due to prolonged exposure to glucocorticoids such as cortisol.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the cause, and may involve surgery to remove tumors. Medications can also be used to control cortisol production.

description: A chalazion (; plural chalazia or chalazions) or meibomian cyst is not a cyst but a granuloma in the eyelid that results from a blocked meibomian gland.
treatment_summary: Warm compresses can help to drain the cyst. Topical medications or surgical removal may be necessary for persistent cases.

description: Cystic fibrosis (CF) is a genetic disorder inherited in an autosomal recessive manner that impairs the normal clearance of mucus from the lungs, which facilitates the colonization and infection of the lungs by bacteria, notably Pseudomonas aeruginosa and Staphylococcus aureus.

risk_factors.label: Both parents are carriers of CFTR mutation

treatment_summary: Treatment involves a combination of therapies to manage lung infections and improve mucus clearance. Nutritional support is also important.

description: Cysticercosis is a tissue infection caused by the young form of the pork tapeworm.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antiparasitic medications to kill the larvae. Surgery may be required to remove cysts in some cases.

description: A urinary tract infection (UTI) is an infection that affects a part of the urinary tract, which includes the bladder, urethra and the kidney.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves antibiotics to eliminate the bacterial infection. Supportive care includes increased fluid intake.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: De Quervain syndrome or De Quervain tendinopathy occurs when two tendons that control movement of the thumb become enlarged where they pass through their tendon sheath in the wrist.

risk_factors.label: Direct injury to the wrist or tendon

treatment_summary: Treatment involves rest, splinting, and anti-inflammatory medications. Physical therapy or corticosteroid injections may also be used.

description: Pressure ulcers, also known as pressure sores, bed sores or pressure injuries, are localised damage to the skin and/or underlying tissue that usually occur over a bony prominence as a result of usually long-term pressure, or pressure in combination with shear or friction.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves relieving pressure on the affected area and wound care. Surgery may be necessary for severe ulcers.

description: Deep vein thrombosis (DVT) is a type of venous thrombosis involving the formation of a blood clot in a deep vein, most commonly in the legs or pelvis.

risk_factors.label: Hormone therapy or birth control pills

treatment_summary: Treatment involves anticoagulant medications to prevent clot growth and new clot formation. Compression stockings can help reduce swelling.

related_diseases.disease: envenomation from spider or animal bite

description: Degenerative disc disease (DDD) is a medical condition typically brought on by the aging process in which there are anatomic changes and possibly a loss of function of one or more intervertebral discs of the spine.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes pain management, physical therapy, and lifestyle modifications. Surgery may be considered in severe cases.

description: Delirium (formerly acute confusional state, an ambiguous term that is now discouraged) is a specific state of acute confusion attributable to the direct physiological consequence of a medical condition, effects of a psychoactive substance, or multiple causes, which usually develops over the course of hours to days.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on addressing the underlying cause and managing symptoms. Supportive care is essential to ensure patient safety and comfort.

description: Dementia is a syndrome, often associated with neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's, and characterized by a general decline in cognitive abilities that affects a person's ability to perform everyday activities.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes medications to manage symptoms and therapies to improve cognitive function. Supportive care and palliative measures help maintain quality of life.

description: Dengue fever is a mosquito-borne disease caused by dengue virus, prevalent in tropical and subtropical areas.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms, preventing dehydration, and monitoring for complications. There is no specific antiviral treatment.

description: Tooth decay, also known as caries, is the breakdown of teeth due to acids produced by bacteria.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes fluoride application, fillings, and root canals to remove decay and restore teeth. Good oral hygiene practices are essential for prevention.

description: Depression may refer to:
treatment_summary: Treatment commonly involves antidepressant medications and psychotherapy. Lifestyle changes, such as exercise and social engagement, can also be beneficial.

description: Atopic dermatitis (AD), also known as atopic eczema, is a long-term type of inflammation of the skin.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes topical corticosteroids and emollients to reduce inflammation and itching. Avoiding sun exposure and using sunscreen are essential preventative measures.

description: Developmental disability is a diverse group of chronic conditions, comprising mental or physical impairments that arise before adulthood.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves therapies to improve physical, cognitive, and social skills. Supportive care focuses on maximizing independence and quality of life.

description: Nasal septum deviation is a physical disorder of the nose, involving a displacement of the nasal septum.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include medications to manage symptoms and surgery to correct the deviation. Septoplasty is a common surgical procedure.

description: Diabetes mellitus, commonly known as diabetes, is a group of common endocrine diseases characterized by sustained high blood sugar levels.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves medications to lower blood sugar, lifestyle changes such as diet and exercise, and regular monitoring. Insulin therapy may be necessary for some patients.

description: Diabetes insipidus (DI) is a condition characterized by large amounts of dilute urine and increased thirst.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on replacing fluids and administering medications to reduce urine output. Desmopressin is a common medication used to treat diabetes insipidus.

description: Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) is a potentially life-threatening acute complication of diabetes mellitus.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on correcting dehydration, electrolyte imbalances, and high blood sugar levels with intravenous fluids, insulin therapy, and electrolyte replacement.

description: Diabetic nephropathy, also known as diabetic kidney disease, is the chronic loss of kidney function occurring in those with diabetes mellitus.
treatment_summary: Management involves controlling blood sugar and blood pressure, along with lifestyle modifications and medications to protect the kidneys. Dialysis or kidney transplant may be necessary in advanced cases.

description: Diabetic neuropathy includes various types of nerve damage associated with diabetes mellitus.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes managing blood sugar levels, pain relief medications, physical therapy, and lifestyle adjustments like smoking cessation and proper foot care.

description: Diabetic retinopathy (also known as diabetic eye disease) is a medical condition in which damage occurs to the retina due to diabetes.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include laser surgery, injections, or vitrectomy to help seal leaking blood vessels, reduce swelling, and prevent further vision loss. Regular eye exams are crucial for early detection and management.

description: Vixen are an American all-female glam rock band formed in Saint Paul, Minnesota, in 1973.
treatment_summary: Diaper rash is typically treated with topical barrier creams and by keeping the area clean and dry. Frequent diaper changes are important.

description: A joint dislocation, also called luxation, occurs when there is an abnormal separation in the joint, where two or more bones meet.
treatment_summary: Ankle dislocations require prompt medical attention to realign the joint, often followed by immobilization with a cast or splint. Physical therapy is essential for rehabilitation.

description: The elbow is the region between the upper arm and the forearm that surrounds the elbow joint.
treatment_summary: Elbow dislocations require prompt reduction to realign the joint, often followed by immobilization. Physical therapy helps restore range of motion and strength.

description: A joint dislocation, also called luxation, occurs when there is an abnormal separation in the joint, where two or more bones meet.
treatment_summary: Finger dislocations typically require manual reduction to realign the joint. Splinting or buddy taping may be needed to stabilize the finger during healing.

description: A joint dislocation, also called luxation, occurs when there is an abnormal separation in the joint, where two or more bones meet.
treatment_summary: Foot dislocations require immediate medical attention for reduction and stabilization, often with surgery. Physical therapy is crucial for regaining function.

description: A hip dislocation refers to a condition in which the thighbone (femur) separates from the hip bone (pelvis).
treatment_summary: Hip dislocations are orthopedic emergencies requiring prompt reduction, often under anesthesia. Physical therapy is essential for regaining strength and mobility.

description: A knee dislocation is an injury in which there is disruption of the knee joint between the tibia and the femur.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes reducing the dislocation, often surgically stabilizing the knee, and physical therapy to regain strength and range of motion. Supportive care helps manage pain and swelling.

description: A patellar dislocation is a knee injury in which the patella (kneecap) slips out of its normal position.
treatment_summary: Initial treatment involves reducing the dislocated patella and immobilization. Physical therapy helps strengthen muscles and improve stability; surgery may be needed for recurrent dislocations.

description: A dislocated shoulder is a condition in which the head of the humerus is detached from the glenoid fossa.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves reducing the dislocation, followed by immobilization and pain management. Physical therapy is crucial to restore strength and range of motion; surgery may be considered for recurrent dislocations.

description: Spondylolisthesis refers to a condition in which one spinal vertebra slips out of place compared to another.
treatment_summary: Treatment ranges from conservative management with physical therapy and pain medication, to surgical stabilization. The approach depends on the severity of the slip and the patient's symptoms.

description: A joint dislocation, also called luxation, occurs when there is an abnormal separation in the joint, where two or more bones meet.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves reducing the dislocation, immobilization, and pain management. Physical therapy helps restore function; surgery may be required for severe cases.

description: Dissociative disorders (DDs) are a range of mental disorders characterized by significant disruptions or fragmentation "in the normal integration of consciousness, memory, identity, emotion, perception, body representation, motor control, and behavior." Dissociative disorders involve involuntary dissociation as an unconscious defense mechanism, wherein the individual with a dissociative disorder experiences separation in these areas as a means to protect against traumatic stress.
treatment_summary: Treatment primarily involves psychotherapy to address the underlying trauma and integrate fragmented aspects of identity. Medication may be used to manage associated symptoms like depression or anxiety.

description: Diverticulitis, also called colonic diverticulitis, is a gastrointestinal disease characterized by inflammation of abnormal pouches—diverticula—that can develop in the wall of the large intestine.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes antibiotics for infection, pain management, and dietary modifications. Surgery may be necessary for severe or recurrent cases.

description: Diverticulosis is the condition of having multiple pouches (diverticula) in the colon that are not inflamed.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on preventing progression to diverticulitis. A high-fiber diet and increased fluid intake are typically recommended; medication is sometimes needed to manage symptoms.

description: Down syndrome or sometimes Down's syndrome, also known as Trisomy 21, is a genetic disorder caused by the presence of all or part of a third copy of chromosome 21.
treatment_summary: Down syndrome management involves therapies to support physical, intellectual, and social development. Supportive care focuses on managing associated health conditions and maximizing quality of life.

description: Substance misuse, also known as drug misuse or, in older vernacular, substance abuse, is the use of a drug in amounts or by methods that are harmful to the individual or others.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes behavioral therapy to address addiction patterns, medication to manage withdrawal symptoms and cravings, and lifestyle changes to support recovery. Comprehensive support systems are also essential.

description: Barbiturates are a class of depressant drugs that are chemically derived from barbituric acid.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing withdrawal symptoms and providing behavioral therapies to prevent relapse. Medications may be used to ease withdrawal, while therapy helps address underlying issues.

description: Crack cocaine, commonly known simply as crack, and also known as rock, is a free base form of the stimulant cocaine that can be smoked.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes behavioral therapies to address addiction and prevent relapse. Medication can help manage withdrawal and cravings, coupled with supportive care.

description: Methamphetamine is a central nervous system (CNS) stimulant that is primarily used as a recreational or performance-enhancing drug and less commonly as a second-line treatment for attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD).
treatment_summary: Treatment involves behavioral therapies to manage addiction and prevent relapse. Medications may be used to reduce cravings and withdrawal symptoms, along with supportive care.

description: The opioid epidemic, also referred to as the opioid crisis, is the rapid increase in the overuse, misuse or abuse, and overdose deaths attributed either in part or in whole to the class of drugs called opiates or opioids since the 1990s.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on medication-assisted therapy to manage withdrawal and cravings, combined with behavioral therapies to prevent relapse. Supportive care addresses underlying issues and promotes recovery.

description: A drug overdose (overdose or OD) is the ingestion or application of a drug or other substance in quantities much greater than are recommended.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves supportive care to stabilize the patient and manage symptoms. Specific antidotes may be administered if available, and vital signs are closely monitored.

description: An adverse drug reaction (ADR) is an unintended medical event, which can potentially be associated with taking a medication.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves stopping the offending medication and providing supportive care to manage symptoms. Antihistamines or corticosteroids may be used to reduce allergic reactions.

description: Drug withdrawal, drug withdrawal syndrome, or substance withdrawal syndrome is the group of symptoms that occur upon the abrupt discontinuation or decrease in the intake of pharmaceutical or recreational drugs.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to manage withdrawal symptoms with medications and supportive care. Behavioral therapies can help with coping strategies and prevent relapse.

description: Computer vision syndrome (CVS) is a condition resulting from focusing the eyes on a computer or other display device for protracted, uninterrupted periods of time and the eye's muscles being unable to recover from the constant tension required to maintain focus on a close object.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on lubricating the eyes with artificial tears and reducing environmental irritants. Lifestyle changes, such as taking breaks from screen time, are also important.

description: Dumping syndrome occurs when food, especially sugar, moves too quickly from the stomach to the duodenum—the first part of the small intestine—in the upper gastrointestinal (GI) tract.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves dietary changes to reduce sugar and refined carbohydrates. Medications can slow gastric emptying, and surgery is sometimes needed for severe cases.

description: Dyshidrosis is a type of dermatitis, characterized by itchy vesicles of 1–2 mm in size, on the palms of the hands, sides of fingers, or bottoms of the feet.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes topical corticosteroids to reduce inflammation and itching. Oral medications or light therapy may be used for severe cases, along with physical therapy.

description: Dysthymia ( dihss-THY-mee-ə), known as persistent depressive disorder (PDD) in the DSM-5-TR and dysthymic disorder in ICD-11, is a psychiatric condition marked by symptoms that are similar to those of major depressive disorder, but which persist for at least two years in adults and one year among pediatric populations.
treatment_summary: Treatment often includes antidepressant medication and psychotherapy, such as cognitive behavioral therapy. These treatments aim to improve mood and develop coping mechanisms.

description: Dolly Sinatra (Italian pronunciation: [siˈnaːtra]; born Natalina Maria Vittoria Garaventa; [nataˈliːna maˈriːa ɡaraˈvɛnta]; December 26, 1894 – January 6, 1977) was an Italian-American homemaker, known as the mother of American singer Frank Sinatra.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the severity and cause. Options include antibiotics for infection, pain management, monitoring, or surgery to repair the eardrum.

description: Earwax, also known by the medical term cerumen, is a waxy substance secreted in the ear canal of humans and other mammals.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves softening and removing the earwax. This can be done with ear drops or by a healthcare professional.

description: An eating disorder (ED) is a mental disorder defined by abnormal eating behaviors that adversely affect a person's physical or mental health.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes therapy to address underlying psychological issues, nutritional counseling, and medical monitoring. Medication may be used to manage co-occurring conditions.

description: Ectopic pregnancy is a complication of pregnancy in which the embryo attaches outside the uterus.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves medication (methotrexate) to stop the growth of the ectopic pregnancy or surgical removal of the ectopic pregnancy. This is done to prevent life threatening complications.

description: Ectropion is a medical condition in which the lower eyelid turns outwards.
treatment_summary: Treatment often includes lubricating eye drops or ointment to alleviate dryness. Surgery may be required to correct the position of the eyelid.

description: Dermatitis  is a term used for different types of skin inflammation, typically characterized by itchiness, redness and a rash.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on relieving itching and inflammation with topical corticosteroids and emollients. Identifying and avoiding triggers is also important.

description: Trisomy 18, also known as Edwards syndrome, is a genetic disorder caused by the presence of a third copy of all or part of chromosome 18.
treatment_summary: Treatment is primarily supportive, focusing on managing symptoms and providing comfort. There is no cure, and the prognosis is poor.

related_diseases.disease: syndrome of inappropriate secretion of adh (siadh)

description: Emphysema is a condition of the lung marked by abnormal enlargement of the alveoli with loss of pulmonary elasticity.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to manage symptoms and slow progression. This includes bronchodilators, pulmonary rehabilitation, and smoking cessation.

description: An empyema (; from Ancient Greek  ἐμπύημα (empúēma) 'abscess') is a collection or gathering of pus within a naturally existing anatomical cavity.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves draining the pus from the pleural space and administering antibiotics to fight the infection. Surgery may be necessary to remove thickened scar tissue.

description: Encephalitis (US: , UK: ) is inflammation of the brain.

risk_factors.label: Geographic location (exposure to mosquitoes or ticks)

treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and reducing brain swelling. Antiviral medications may be used in some cases.

description: Endocarditis is an inflammation of the inner layer of the heart, the endocardium.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves long-term antibiotic therapy to eradicate the infection. Surgery may be needed to repair or replace damaged heart valves.

description: Endometrial cancer is a cancer that arises from the endometrium (the lining of the uterus or womb).

risk_factors.label: Family history of endometrial, colon, or ovarian cancer

treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves hysterectomy, often combined with radiation therapy and/or chemotherapy. Hormone therapy may also be used in some cases.

description: Endometrial hyperplasia is a condition of excessive proliferation of the cells of the endometrium, or inner lining of the uterus.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include progestin therapy to balance hormone levels, or a hysterectomy. Regular monitoring is important to detect any cancerous changes.

description: Endometriosis is a disease in which tissue similar to the lining of the uterus grows elsewhere in the body.
treatment_summary: Pain medications, hormone therapy, and surgery can help manage symptoms and remove endometrial tissue. Hysterectomy is sometimes considered in severe cases.

description: Endophthalmitis, or endophthalmia, is inflammation of the interior cavity of the eye, usually caused by an infection.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves injecting antibiotics or antifungals directly into the eye. In some cases, surgery may be necessary to remove infected tissue.

disease: envenomation from spider or animal bite
description: A spider bite, also known as arachnidism, is an injury resulting from the bite of a spider.

risk_factors.label: Geographic location (certain areas have more venomous specie

treatment_summary: Treatment includes wound care, pain management, and observation for systemic symptoms. Antivenom is available for some bites and can reduce symptom duration.

description: An ependymoma is a tumor that arises from the ependyma, a tissue of the central nervous system.

risk_factors.label: Prior radiation exposure to the brain or spine

treatment_summary: The primary treatment is surgical removal of the tumor, if possible. Radiation therapy and chemotherapy may also be used.

description: Epididymitis is a medical condition characterized by inflammation of the epididymis, a curved structure at the back of the testicle.
treatment_summary: Antibiotics are the primary treatment for bacterial infections. Pain relievers and supportive measures, such as rest and scrotal elevation, can also help.

description: Epidural hematoma is when bleeding occurs between the tough outer membrane covering the brain (dura mater) and the skull.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves surgical evacuation of the hematoma to relieve pressure on the brain. Monitoring for neurological changes is crucial.

description: Epilepsy is a group of neurological disorders characterized by a tendency for recurrent, unprovoked seizures.
treatment_summary: Epilepsy is often managed with medication to control seizures. In some cases, surgery or lifestyle adjustments may be necessary.
