description: Erectile dysfunction (ED), also referred to as impotence, is a form of sexual dysfunction in males characterized by the persistent or recurring inability to achieve or maintain a penile erection with sufficient rigidity and duration for satisfactory sexual activity.
treatment_summary: Erectile dysfunction can be treated with oral medications and lifestyle changes. Counseling and therapy may also be beneficial.

description: Erythema multiforme (EM) is an immune-mediated inflammatory skin condition associated with several viral infections, that appears with red patches evolving into target lesions, typically on both hands.
treatment_summary: Erythema multiforme treatment focuses on managing symptoms and addressing the underlying cause. Topical or oral medications may be used to reduce inflammation.

description: Esophageal cancer (American English) or oesophageal cancer (British English) is cancer arising from the esophagus—the food pipe that runs between the throat and the stomach.
treatment_summary: Esophageal cancer treatment may involve surgery, chemotherapy, and radiation therapy. Palliative care is often used to manage symptoms.

description: Esophageal varices are extremely dilated sub-mucosal veins in the lower third of the esophagus.
treatment_summary: Esophageal varices are treated to prevent bleeding. Treatments include medications and surgical interventions to reduce pressure in the veins.

description: Esophagitis, also spelled oesophagitis, is a disease characterized by inflammation of the esophagus.
treatment_summary: Esophagitis is typically treated with medications to reduce stomach acid production. Lifestyle changes can also help manage symptoms.

description: Essential tremor (ET), also called benign tremor, familial tremor, and idiopathic tremor, is a medical condition characterized by involuntary rhythmic contractions and relaxations (oscillations or twitching movements) of certain muscle groups in one or more body parts of unknown cause.
treatment_summary: Essential tremor can be managed with medications to reduce tremor severity. Physical therapy and, in some cases, surgery may also be options.

description: Eustachian tube dysfunction (ETD) is a disorder where pressure abnormalities in the middle ear result in symptoms.
treatment_summary: Eustachian tube dysfunction is often treated with decongestants and other medications to relieve congestion. In some cases, tubes may be surgically inserted.

description: Extrapyramidal symptoms (EPS) are symptoms that are archetypically associated with the extrapyramidal system of the brain.
treatment_summary: Extrapyramidal symptoms are managed by adjusting medication dosages or adding medications to counteract the side effects. Supportive care is also important.

description:
Cockeyed may refer to:

Crooked or askew; not level
An eye alignment disorder, Strabismus
A website, Cockeyed.com
Louis Fratto (1907-1967), American labor racketeer and organized crime figure nicknamed "Cock-eyed"
treatment_summary: Eye alignment disorders can be corrected with surgery to realign the eye muscles. Vision therapy can also help improve eye coordination.

description: A factitious disorder is a mental disorder in which a person, without a malingering motive, acts as if they have an illness by deliberately producing, feigning, or exaggerating symptoms, purely to attain (for themselves or for another) a patient's role.
treatment_summary: Psychotherapy, especially cognitive behavioral therapy, is often used to address underlying psychological issues. Medications may be used to treat co-occurring mental health conditions.

description: In mammals, the vulva (pl.: vulvas or vulvae) comprises mostly external, visible structures of the female genitalia leading into the interior of the female reproductive tract.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves topical or oral medications to combat the infection. Maintaining good hygiene is also important.

description: Female infertility refers to infertility in women, which is the inability to conceive after sexual intercourse.
treatment_summary: Treatment may include medications to stimulate ovulation or surgical procedures to address structural issues. Assisted reproductive technologies may also be considered.

description: Fetal alcohol spectrum disorders (FASDs) are a group of conditions that can occur in a person who is exposed to alcohol during gestation.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and providing supportive care to improve development and quality of life. Therapy and specialized education can help address cognitive and behavioral challenges.

description: Fibroadenomas are benign breast tumours characterized by an admixture of stromal and epithelial tissue.
treatment_summary: Many fibroadenomas do not require treatment and are monitored through regular breast exams. Surgical removal may be considered if the fibroadenoma is large or causing discomfort.

description: Fibrocystic breast changes is a condition of the breasts where there may be pain, breast cysts, and breast masses.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on pain relief and may include pain relievers or hormonal medications. Supportive measures like wearing a supportive bra can also help.

description: Fibromyalgia (FM) is a long-term adverse health condition characterised by widespread chronic pain.

risk_factors.label: Co-existing conditions like lupus or arthritis

treatment_summary: Treatment involves a combination of medications for pain management, physical therapy to improve strength and flexibility, and behavioral therapy to cope with chronic pain. Lifestyle changes, such as exercise and stress reduction, are also important.

description: Flat feet, also called pes planus or fallen arches, is a postural deformity in which the arches of the foot collapse, with the entire sole of the foot coming into complete or near-complete contact with the ground.

risk_factors.label: Injury to the foot or ankle

treatment_summary: Treatment may include arch supports, physical therapy to strengthen foot muscles, and in some cases, surgery. Supportive footwear and weight management are also recommended.

description: Floaters or eye floaters are sometimes-visible deposits (e.g., the shadows of tiny structures of protein or other cell debris projected onto the retina) within the eye's vitreous humour ("the vitreous"), which is normally transparent, or between the vitreous and retina.
treatment_summary: Most floaters are harmless and do not require treatment; however, if they significantly impair vision, a vitrectomy (surgical removal of the vitreous humor) may be considered. Regular eye exams are important to monitor for any changes.

related_diseases.disease: retinopathy due to high blood pressure

description: Influenza, commonly known as the flu, is an infectious disease caused by influenza viruses.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves antiviral medications to reduce the severity and duration of symptoms, as well as supportive care, such as rest and fluids. Vaccination is the best preventative measure.

description: Hypervolemia, also known as fluid overload, is the medical condition where there is too much fluid in the blood.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves reducing fluid intake and using diuretics to increase fluid excretion. Dietary changes, such as limiting sodium, are also important.

description: Folate deficiency, also known as vitamin B9 deficiency, is a low level of folate and derivatives in the body.
treatment_summary: Treatment primarily involves oral folate supplementation to restore normal levels. Dietary changes to include folate-rich foods are also recommended.

description: A food allergy is an abnormal immune response to food.
treatment_summary: Management involves avoiding the allergenic food and using antihistamines to relieve mild reactions. Epinephrine is required for severe allergic reactions.

description: A foreign body (FB) is any object originating outside the body of an organism.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves removing the foreign object from the ear canal. This is typically done manually or with specialized instruments.

description: A foreign body (FB) is any object originating outside the body of an organism.
treatment_summary: The foreign body must be removed from the eye to prevent further damage. Irrigation or manual extraction is often used.

disease: foreign body in the gastrointestinal tract
description: Rectal foreign bodies are large foreign items found in the rectum that can be assumed to have been inserted through the anus, rather than reaching the rectum via the mouth and gastrointestinal tract.
treatment_summary: Management typically involves endoscopic or surgical removal of the foreign body. Observation may be appropriate if the object is small and non-toxic.

description: A foreign body (FB) is any object originating outside the body of an organism.
treatment_summary: Treatment requires removal of the foreign object from the nasal cavity. This is usually done with specialized instruments.

description: Tonsil stones, also known as tonsilloliths, are mineralizations of debris within the crevices of the tonsils.
treatment_summary: Tonsil stones can usually be dislodged with gargling or manual removal. Larger stones may require removal by a healthcare professional.

description: Vaginal foreign body refers to the presence of a foreign object or substance within the vagina.
treatment_summary: Treatment requires removal of the foreign body. A pelvic exam will be required.

description: An ankle fracture is a break of one or more of the bones that make up the ankle joint.
treatment_summary: Treatment may involve immobilization with a cast or surgery to stabilize the bones. Physical therapy is often needed for rehabilitation.

description: In human anatomy, the arm refers to the upper limb in common usage, although academically the term specifically means the upper arm between the glenohumeral joint (shoulder joint) and the elbow joint.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves immobilizing the arm with a cast or splint. Surgery may be needed for severe fractures.

description: Facial trauma, also called maxillofacial trauma, is any physical trauma to the face.
treatment_summary: Treatment may include surgery to realign the bones. Supportive care is provided to manage pain and swelling.

description: A broken finger or finger fracture is a common type of bone fracture, affecting a finger.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves splinting or casting the finger. Surgery may be necessary for complex fractures.

description: The foot (pl.: feet) is an anatomical structure found in many vertebrates.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves immobilization with a cast or boot. Physical therapy is often needed after healing.

description: A hand is a prehensile, multi-fingered appendage located at the end of the forearm or forelimb of primates such as humans, chimpanzees, monkeys, and lemurs.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves splinting, casting, or surgery. Physical therapy helps restore function.

description: Mandibular fracture, also known as fracture of the jaw, is a break through the mandibular bone.
treatment_summary: Treatment often requires surgery to realign and stabilize the jaw. A liquid diet may be necessary during healing.

description: The human leg is the lower limb of the human body, including the thigh, knee, lower leg, ankle, foot, or sometimes even the hip or buttock region.
treatment_summary: Treatment may involve casting, surgery with rods or plates, and physical therapy. Weight-bearing restrictions are common during healing.

description: A cervical fracture, commonly called a broken neck, is a fracture of any of the seven cervical vertebrae in the neck.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery to stabilize the spine. Immobilization with a brace is also necessary.

description: A patella fracture is a break of the kneecap.
treatment_summary: Treatment can involve immobilization with a cast or brace, or surgery. Physical therapy is crucial for regaining knee function.

description: A pelvic fracture is a break of the bony structure of the pelvis.
treatment_summary: Treatment ranges from supportive care to surgery depending on severity. Physical therapy is used for rehabilitation.

description: A rib fracture is a break in a rib bone.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on pain management and allowing the bone to heal.  Surgery is sometimes needed for severe fractures.

description: A scapular fracture is a fracture of the scapula, the shoulder blade.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes pain management, immobilization, and physical therapy. Surgery is sometimes necessary for complex fractures.

description: A skull fracture is a break in one or more of the eight bones that form the cranial portion of the skull, usually occurring as a result of blunt force trauma.
treatment_summary: Treatment may include monitoring for complications like bleeding, surgery to repair the fracture, and medication for pain.

description: A compression fracture is a collapse of a vertebra.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on pain control, preventing further damage, and improving function. Options include pain medication, physical therapy, bracing, or surgery.

description: Friedreich's ataxia (FRDA) is a rare, inherited, autosomal recessive neurodegenerative disorder that primarily affects the nervous system, causing progressive damage to the spinal cord, peripheral nerves, and cerebellum, leading to impaired muscle coordination (ataxia).
treatment_summary: There is no cure, so treatment focuses on managing symptoms and improving quality of life with physical therapy and medication.

description: Frostbite is an injury to skin or other living tissue that is allowed to freeze, especially affecting the fingers, toes, nose, ears, cheeks and chin.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves rewarming the affected area, pain management, and preventing infection. In severe cases, surgery may be needed to remove damaged tissue.

description: Dermatophytosis, also known as tinea and ringworm, is a fungal infection of the skin (a dermatomycosis), that may affect skin, hair, and nails.

risk_factors.label: Contact with infected individuals or animals

treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves antifungal shampoos or oral medications to eliminate the fungal infection.  Prevention focuses on good hygiene.

description: Fungal infection, also known as mycosis, is a disease caused by fungi.

risk_factors.label: Contact with infected individuals or animals

treatment_summary: Treatment usually includes topical antifungal creams or oral medications, along with improved hygiene practices. Keeping the skin clean and dry is helpful.

description: Glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency (G6PDD), also known as favism, is the most common enzyme deficiency anemia worldwide.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on avoiding triggers and managing complications such as anemia.  Blood transfusions might be needed in severe cases.

description: Sarcoidosis, also known as Besnier–Boeck–Schaumann disease, is a non-infectious granulomatous disease involving abnormal collections of inflammatory cells that form lumps known as granulomata.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the underlying cause. It may involve medications to balance hormone levels or address the underlying disease.

description: A gallstone is a stone formed within the gallbladder from precipitated bile components.
treatment_summary: Treatment may include surgery to remove the gallbladder or medications to dissolve the gallstones. Lifestyle changes, such as diet modification, can also help manage symptoms.

description: A ganglion cyst is a fluid-filled bump associated with a joint or tendon sheath.
treatment_summary: Many ganglion cysts require no treatment and can be monitored. If symptomatic, aspiration or surgical removal can be considered.

description: Gas gangrene (also known as clostridial myonecrosis) is a bacterial infection that produces tissue gas in gangrene.
treatment_summary: Gas gangrene requires immediate treatment with surgical debridement and antibiotics. Supportive care is also essential to manage complications.

description: Gastritis is the inflammation of the lining of the stomach.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes medications to reduce stomach acid and treat H. pylori infection, if present. Lifestyle changes such as avoiding alcohol and certain foods can help manage symptoms.

description: Peptic ulcer disease refers to damage of the inner part of the stomach's gastric mucosa (lining of the stomach), the first part of the small intestine, or sometimes the lower esophagus.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves medications to reduce stomach acid and antibiotics to eradicate H. pylori. Lifestyle changes, like avoiding NSAIDs and alcohol, are important.

description: Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) or gastro-oesophageal reflux disease (GORD)  is a chronic upper gastrointestinal disease in which stomach content persistently and regularly flows up into the esophagus, resulting in symptoms and/or complications.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes lifestyle changes, medications to reduce stomach acid, and sometimes surgery. Elevating the head of the bed and avoiding trigger foods can also help.

description: Gastrointestinal bleeding (GI bleed), also called gastrointestinal hemorrhage (GIB), is all forms of bleeding in the gastrointestinal tract, from the mouth to the rectum.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on stopping the bleeding, often with medications or endoscopic procedures, and supportive care. Identifying and treating the underlying cause is crucial.

description: Gastroparesis (gastro- from Ancient Greek γαστήρ – gaster, "stomach"; and -paresis, πάρεσις – "partial paralysis") is a medical disorder of ineffective neuromuscular contractions (peristalsis) of the stomach, resulting in food and liquid remaining in the stomach for a prolonged period.
treatment_summary: Management includes dietary modifications, medications to improve gastric emptying, and symptom management. Severe cases may require nutritional support.

description: Genital herpes is a herpes infection of the genitals caused by the herpes simplex virus (HSV).
treatment_summary: Genital herpes is treated with antiviral medications to reduce the frequency and severity of outbreaks. There is currently no cure.

description: Gestational diabetes is a condition in which an individual without diabetes develops high blood sugar levels during pregnancy.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves dietary changes, exercise, and monitoring blood sugar levels. Some individuals may require insulin injections to manage blood sugar levels.

description: Glaucoma is a group of eye diseases that can lead to damage of the optic nerve, which transmits visual information from the eye to the brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on lowering intraocular pressure to prevent further optic nerve damage. This can be achieved through eye drops, oral medications, laser treatment, or surgery.

description: Glucocorticoid deficiency is a condition where the body doesn't produce enough glucocorticoid hormones.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves hormone replacement therapy with glucocorticoids to restore normal hormone levels. Dosage is adjusted based on individual needs and response.

description: A goitre (British English), or goiter (American English), is a swelling in the neck resulting from an enlarged thyroid gland.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the size and cause of the goiter, ranging from observation to medication or surgery. Iodine supplementation may be necessary in cases of deficiency.

related_diseases.disease: foreign body in the gastrointestinal tract

description: Gonorrhea or gonorrhoea, colloquially known as the clap, is a sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by the bacterium Neisseria gonorrhoeae.
treatment_summary: Gonorrhea is treated with antibiotics to eliminate the bacterial infection. It is important to treat all sexual partners to prevent re-infection and further spread.

description: Gout ( GOWT) is a form of inflammatory arthritis characterized by recurrent attacks of pain in a red, tender, hot, and swollen joint, caused by the deposition of needle-shaped crystals of the monosodium salt of uric acid.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves medications to reduce pain and inflammation during acute attacks, and medications to lower uric acid levels long-term. Lifestyle changes, such as diet modification, are also important.

description: Granuloma inguinale  is a bacterial disease caused by Klebsiella granulomatis (formerly known as Calymmatobacterium granulomatis) characterized by genital ulcers.
treatment_summary: Granuloma inguinale is treated with antibiotics to eradicate the Klebsiella granulomatis bacteria.  Prolonged treatment courses may be required to ensure complete resolution.

description: Graves' disease, also known as toxic diffuse goiter or Basedow's disease, is an autoimmune disease that affects the thyroid.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce thyroid hormone production and manage symptoms.  Options include antithyroid medications, radioactive iodine therapy, or thyroid surgery.

description: Guillain–Barré syndrome (GBS) is a rapid-onset muscle weakness caused by the immune system damaging the peripheral nervous system.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on supportive care, such as mechanical ventilation, and therapies to reduce the severity and duration of the illness. These may include plasma exchange or intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIg).

description: Periodontal disease, also known as gum disease, is a set of inflammatory conditions affecting the tissues surrounding the teeth.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves scaling and root planing to remove plaque and tartar, antibiotics to control infection, and possibly surgery to repair damaged tissues. Maintaining good oral hygiene is crucial.

description: Gynecomastia (also spelled gynaecomastia) is the non-cancerous enlargement of one or both breasts in men due to the growth of breast tissue as a result of a hormone imbalance between estrogens and androgens.

risk_factors.label: Medical conditions (e.g., liver or kidney disease)

treatment_summary: Treatment may involve addressing the underlying cause, such as discontinuing certain medications or treating a medical condition.  Medications or surgery may be considered in some cases.

description: A hammer toe, hammertoe or contracted toe is a deformity of the muscles and ligaments of the proximal interphalangeal joint of the second, third, fourth, or fifth toe, bending it into a shape resembling a hammer.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes wearing appropriate shoes, exercises, or surgery to straighten the toe. Supportive care like orthotics may also help.

description: Hashimoto's thyroiditis, also known as chronic lymphocytic thyroiditis, Hashimoto's disease and autoimmune thyroiditis, is an autoimmune disease in which the thyroid gland is gradually destroyed.
treatment_summary: The primary treatment is thyroid hormone replacement medication. Regular monitoring of thyroid hormone levels is also necessary.

description: Head and neck cancer is a general term encompassing multiple cancers that can develop in the head and neck region.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include surgery, radiation therapy, chemotherapy, and targeted therapy. A combination of treatments may be used depending on the cancer stage and location.

description: A head injury is any injury that results in trauma to the skull or brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment ranges from monitoring for mild injuries to surgery for severe cases. Supportive care focuses on managing symptoms and preventing complications.

description: Post-dural-puncture headache (PDPH) is a complication of puncture of the dura mater (one of the membranes around the brain and spinal cord).
treatment_summary: Treatment includes bed rest, hydration, and pain relievers. An epidural blood patch may be necessary in severe cases.

description: A myocardial infarction (MI), commonly known as a heart attack, occurs when blood flow decreases or stops in one of the arteries of the heart, causing infarction (tissue death) to the heart muscle.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on restoring blood flow to the heart, often with medication or surgery. Lifestyle changes are essential for long-term management.

description: Heart block (HB) is a disorder in the heart's rhythm due to a fault in the natural pacemaker.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies depending on the severity of the block, ranging from observation to medication or pacemaker implantation. Pacemakers help regulate heart rhythm.

description: A blunt cardiac injury is an injury to the heart as the result of blunt trauma, typically to the anterior chest wall.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on supportive care and managing complications. Monitoring heart rhythm and function is critical.

description: Heart failure (HF), also known as congestive heart failure (CHF), is a syndrome caused by an impairment in the heart's ability to fill with and pump blood.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes medications, lifestyle changes, and sometimes surgery to improve heart function. Regular monitoring is essential.

description: Heat exhaustion is a heat-related illness characterized by the body's inability to effectively cool itself, typically occurring in high ambient temperatures or during intense physical exertion.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves cooling the body, rehydration, and rest. Moving to a cooler environment and drinking fluids are crucial.

description: Heat stroke or heatstroke, also known as sun-stroke, is a severe heat illness that results in a body temperature greater than 40.0 °C (104.0 °F), along with red skin, headache, dizziness, and confusion.
treatment_summary: Immediate cooling measures are crucial. This includes moving the person to a cooler environment and applying ice packs.

related_diseases.disease: syndrome of inappropriate secretion of adh (siadh)

description: A hemangioma or haemangioma is a usually benign vascular tumor derived from blood vessel cell types.
treatment_summary: Many hemangiomas resolve on their own and require only monitoring. Others may need medication or surgical intervention.

description: Hemarthrosis is a bleeding into joint spaces.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing pain and swelling. Physical therapy and addressing the underlying cause are important.

description: A hematoma, also spelled haematoma, or blood suffusion is a localized bleeding outside of blood vessels, due to either disease or trauma including injury or surgery and may involve blood continuing to seep from broken capillaries.
treatment_summary: Most hematomas resolve on their own with time and supportive care. Larger or problematic hematomas may require drainage.

description: Hemiparesis, also called unilateral paresis, is the weakness of one entire side of the body (hemi- meaning 'half').
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on rehabilitation and improving motor function. This often includes physical and occupational therapy.

description: Iron overload is the abnormal and increased accumulation of total iron in the body, leading to organ damage.
treatment_summary: Treatment primarily involves removing excess iron from the body.  This is commonly done through regular blood removal (phlebotomy).

related_diseases.disease: kidney disease due to longstanding hypertension

description: Hemolytic anemia or haemolytic anaemia is a form of anemia due to hemolysis, the abnormal breakdown of red blood cells (RBCs), either in the blood vessels (intravascular hemolysis) or elsewhere in the human body (extravascular).
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the cause and severity. Options include medications to suppress the immune system or blood transfusions.

description: Haemophilia (British English), or hemophilia (American English) (from Ancient Greek  αἷμα (haîma) 'blood' and  φιλία (philía) 'love of'), is a mostly inherited genetic disorder that impairs the body's ability to make blood clots, a process needed to stop bleeding.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves replacing the missing clotting factor to prevent and control bleeding.  This can be done prophylactically or on demand.

description: Hemorrhoids (or haemorrhoids), also known as piles, are vascular structures in the anal canal.
treatment_summary: Treatment ranges from lifestyle changes to reduce straining to topical medications for symptom relief. In some cases, surgery may be necessary.

description: Hepatic encephalopathy (HE) is an altered level of consciousness as a result of liver failure.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing ammonia levels in the blood and supporting liver function. Dietary changes and medications are often used.

description: Hepatitis is inflammation of the liver tissue.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on removing the toxin and supporting liver function. Monitoring helps to track the liver's recovery.

description: A disc herniation or spinal disc herniation is an injury to the intervertebral disc between two vertebrae, usually caused by excessive strain or trauma to the spine.
treatment_summary: Physical therapy and pain medication are often used initially. Surgery may be necessary in severe cases.

description: Herpangina, also called mouth blisters, is a painful mouth infection caused by coxsackieviruses.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves managing symptoms with pain relievers and ensuring adequate hydration. The illness is typically self-limiting.

description: A hiatal hernia or hiatus hernia is a type of hernia in which abdominal organs (typically the stomach) slip through the diaphragm into the middle compartment of the chest.
treatment_summary: Lifestyle changes and medication can manage mild symptoms. Surgery may be required for severe cases.

description: Hidradenitis suppurativa (HS), sometimes known as acne inversa or Verneuil's disease, is a long-term dermatological condition characterized by the occurrence of inflamed and swollen lumps.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes topical or oral medications to manage inflammation and infection. Surgery may be needed to remove affected areas.

description: Hypertension, also known as high blood pressure, is a long-term medical condition in which the blood pressure in the arteries is persistently elevated.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves lifestyle modifications and medication to lower blood pressure. Regular monitoring is crucial for managing the condition.

description: Hirschsprung's disease (HD or HSCR) is a birth defect in which nerves are missing from parts of the intestine.
treatment_summary: Surgical removal of the affected portion of the intestine is the primary treatment. This allows for normal bowel function.

description: Hirsutism is excessive body hair on parts of the body where hair is normally absent or minimal.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include medications to reduce hair growth and topical creams for hair removal. Underlying hormonal imbalances are often addressed.

description: Histoplasmosis is a fungal infection caused by Histoplasma capsulatum.

risk_factors.label: Exposure to bird or bat droppings

treatment_summary: Antifungal medications are used to treat histoplasmosis. The duration of treatment depends on the severity of the infection.

description: Endocrine diseases are disorders of the endocrine system.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies widely based on the specific disorder. It may include hormone replacement therapy, surgery, or lifestyle changes.

description: Human papillomavirus infection (HPV infection) is an infection caused by a DNA virus from the Papillomaviridae family.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on removing warts and monitoring for cervical changes. Topical medications and surgical procedures are common approaches.

description: Human immunodeficiency viruses (HIVs) are two species of Lentivirus (a subgroup of retrovirus) that infect humans.
treatment_summary: Antiretroviral therapy (ART) is used to control the virus. Supportive care helps manage symptoms and opportunistic infections.

description: Huntington's disease (HD), also known as Huntington's chorea, is a fatal neurodegenerative disease that is usually inherited.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms, as there is no cure. Medications, physical therapy, and supportive care can improve quality of life.

description: A molar pregnancy, also known as a hydatidiform mole, is an abnormal form of pregnancy in which a non-viable fertilized egg implants in the uterus.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves removing the abnormal tissue. Close monitoring is required to ensure complete removal and prevent complications.

description: A hydrocele testis is an accumulation of clear fluid within the cavum vaginale, the potential space between the layers of the tunica vaginalis of the testicle.
treatment_summary: Mild cases may resolve on their own or require monitoring. Surgery may be necessary for large or symptomatic hydroceles.

description: Hydrocephalus is a condition in which cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) builds up within and/or around the brain, which can cause pressure to increase in the skull.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves surgical placement of a shunt to drain excess fluid.  This reduces pressure on the brain.

description: Hydronephrosis is a medical condition where the kidney becomes enlarged due to a complete or partial obstruction in the outflow of urine.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on relieving the obstruction and preventing kidney damage. This may involve surgery or medication.

description: Hypercalcemia, also spelled hypercalcaemia, is a high calcium (Ca2+) level in the blood serum.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the severity and cause, including medications to lower calcium levels. Supportive care such as IV fluids may be needed.

description: High cholesterol, also called Hypercholesterolemia, is the presence of high levels of cholesterol in the blood.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes lifestyle changes like diet and exercise. Medications such as statins may be prescribed to lower cholesterol levels.

description: Hyperemesis gravidarum (HG) is a pregnancy complication that is characterized by severe nausea, vomiting, weight loss, and possibly dehydration.

risk_factors.label: History of HG in previous pregnancies

treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and preventing complications like dehydration. This includes medications, IV fluids, and dietary modifications.

description: Hypergammaglobulinemia is a medical condition with elevated levels of gamma globulin.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing the underlying cause and reducing the gamma globulin levels. Supportive care can help manage symptoms.

description: Hyperhidrosis is a medical condition in which a person exhibits excessive sweating, more than is required for the regulation of body temperature.
treatment_summary: Treatments range from topical antiperspirants to oral medications or surgery. Behavioral therapy can address anxiety-related triggers.

description: Hyperkalemia is an elevated level of potassium (K+) in the blood.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves medications to lower potassium levels and addressing the underlying cause. Dietary changes are also often recommended.

description: Hyperlipidemia is abnormally high levels of any or all lipids (e.g.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes lifestyle changes like diet and exercise, and often oral medications to lower lipid levels.

description: Hypernatremia, also spelled hypernatraemia, is a high concentration of sodium in the blood.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on carefully rehydrating the patient and addressing the underlying cause. Sodium levels must be monitored closely.

description: Far-sightedness, also known as long-sightedness, hypermetropia, and hyperopia, is a condition of the eye where distant objects are seen clearly but near objects appear blurred.
treatment_summary: Corrective lenses or surgery can improve vision. Prescription lenses compensate for the focal point error.

description: Hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state (HHS) is a serious complication of diabetes that happens when blood sugar levels are very high for a long period of time.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves intravenous fluids, insulin, and electrolyte correction. Careful monitoring of blood sugar and electrolytes is crucial.

description: Pre-eclampsia is a multi-system disorder specific to pregnancy, characterized by the new onset of high blood pressure and often a significant amount of protein in the urine (proteinuria) or by the new onset of high blood pressure along with significant end-organ damage, with or without the proteinuria.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on controlling blood pressure with medication and monitoring the mother and baby closely.  In severe cases, early delivery may be necessary.

description: Hypertensive heart disease includes a number of complications of high blood pressure that affect the heart.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes managing blood pressure with medication and adopting a healthy lifestyle.  Surgery may be necessary for certain complications.

description: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM, or HOCM when obstructive) is a condition in which muscle tissues of the heart become thickened without an obvious cause.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce symptoms and prevent complications with medications, lifestyle changes, or surgery. Medications can help to slow the heart rate and allow the ventricle to fill more completely.

description: Hypocalcemia is a medical condition characterized by low calcium levels in the blood serum.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves calcium and vitamin D supplementation, either orally or intravenously depending on severity. Addressing the underlying cause is also important.

description: Hypoglycemia (American English), also spelled hypoglycaemia or hypoglycæmia (British English), sometimes called low blood sugar, is a fall in blood sugar to levels below normal, typically below 70 mg/dL (3.9 mmol/L).
treatment_summary: Immediate treatment involves consuming glucose or simple sugars. Long-term management focuses on addressing the underlying cause and dietary modifications.

description: Hypokalemia is a low level of potassium (K+) in the blood serum.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves potassium supplementation, either orally or intravenously depending on severity. Identifying and managing the underlying cause is also crucial.

description: Hyponatremia or hyponatraemia is a low concentration of sodium in the blood.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves sodium replacement, either orally or intravenously depending on severity. Fluid restriction might be recommended along with addressing the underlying cause.

description: Hypothermia is defined as a body core temperature below 35.0 °C (95.0 °F) in humans.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves rewarming the body through passive and active methods. Supportive care, such as monitoring vital signs, is also essential.

description: Hypothyroidism is an endocrine disease in which the thyroid gland does not produce enough thyroid hormones.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves thyroid hormone replacement therapy with levothyroxine. Regular monitoring of thyroid hormone levels is necessary to adjust the dosage.

description: Hypovolemia, also known as volume depletion or volume contraction, is a state of abnormally low extracellular fluid in the body.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves fluid replacement with intravenous fluids. Identifying and managing the underlying cause of fluid loss is crucial.

description: Epilepsy is a group of neurological disorders characterized by a tendency for recurrent, unprovoked seizures.
treatment_summary: Treatment options range from hormonal medications, like birth control pills, to lifestyle changes. Ruling out underlying medical conditions is important.

description: Hirsutism is excessive body hair on parts of the body where hair is normally absent or minimal.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes hormonal medications to regulate bleeding and manage symptoms. Surgery may be considered for underlying structural problems.

description: Anovulation is when the ovaries do not release an oocyte during a menstrual cycle.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing underlying conditions like PCOS and regulating menstrual cycles with hormonal medications. Lifestyle modifications, such as weight management and stress reduction, may be helpful.

description: Heavy menstrual bleeding (HMB), previously known as menorrhagia or hematomunia, is a menstrual period with excessively heavy flow.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on regulating hormone levels with oral contraceptives or other medications. Lifestyle adjustments, such as stress reduction and dietary changes, can also help manage symptoms.

description: Abnormal uterine bleeding (menometrorrhagia) is bleeding between monthly periods, prolonged bleeding or an extremely heavy period. Possible causes include fibroids, polyps, hormone changes and, in rare cases, cancer.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves hormonal medications to regulate bleeding or surgical procedures to address structural abnormalities like fibroids or polyps. Regular monitoring helps to identify and manage any underlying causes.

description: Edema (American English), also spelled oedema (Commonwealth English), and also known as fluid retention, swelling, dropsy and hydropsy, is the build-up of fluid in the body's tissue.
treatment_summary: Pain relievers and hormonal birth control can manage pain and regulate cycles. Lifestyle changes, such as exercise and heat application, may also provide relief.

description: Ileus is a disruption of the normal propulsive ability of the intestine.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves bowel rest, intravenous fluids, and sometimes nasogastric suction. Surgery may be necessary if the ileus is caused by a mechanical obstruction.

description: Impetigo is a contagious bacterial infection that involves the superficial skin.
treatment_summary: Impetigo is treated with topical antibiotics for mild cases. More severe cases may require oral antibiotics.

description: Impulse-control disorder (ICD) is a class of psychiatric disorders characterized by impulsivity – failure to resist a temptation, an urge, or an impulse; or having the inability to not speak on a thought.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves cognitive-behavioral therapy to manage impulses and medication to address underlying psychiatric conditions. Support groups and lifestyle adjustments may also be helpful.

description: Indigestion, also known as dyspepsia or upset stomach, is a condition of impaired digestion.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes over-the-counter antacids and acid reducers. Lifestyle modifications, such as avoiding trigger foods and reducing stress, can also help.

description: Abortion is the termination of a pregnancy by removal or expulsion of an embryo or fetus.
treatment_summary: Induced abortion can be performed surgically or with medication. Counseling and supportive care are often provided.

description: A staphylococcal infection or staph infection is an infection caused by members of the Staphylococcus genus of bacteria.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antibiotics, either topical or oral, to combat the infection. In some cases, surgical debridement may be necessary to remove infected tissue.

related_diseases.disease: envenomation from spider or animal bite

description: Gastroenteritis, also known as infectious diarrhea, is an inflammation of the gastrointestinal tract including the stomach and intestine.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on rehydration with oral fluids and electrolyte replacement. Anti-diarrheal medications may be used in some cases, but antibiotics are generally not necessary unless a bacterial cause is identified.

description: An ingrown nail, also known as onychocryptosis, from Ancient Greek ὄνυξ (ónux), meaning "nail", and κρυπτός (kruptós), meaning "hidden", is a common form of nail disease.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves trimming or removing the portion of the nail that is growing into the skin. Topical antibiotics may be used to prevent infection.

description: An inguinal hernia or groin hernia is a hernia (protrusion) of abdominal cavity contents through the inguinal canal.
treatment_summary: Surgical repair is often required to push the protruding tissue back into place and strengthen the abdominal wall. Monitoring may be appropriate for small, asymptomatic hernias.

description: A sprained ankle (twisted ankle, rolled ankle, turned ankle, etc.) is an injury where sprain occurs on one or more ligaments of the ankle.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves rest, ice, compression, and elevation (RICE) to reduce swelling and pain. Physical therapy helps restore strength and range of motion.

description: Internal bleeding (also called internal haemorrhage) is a loss of blood from a blood vessel that collects inside the body, and is not usually visible from the outside.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the severity and location of the bleeding, and may involve surgery to repair damaged vessels. Supportive care, such as blood transfusions, may also be necessary.

description: An abdomen (also belly or stomach in vertebrates, or metasoma in arthropods)  is the front part of the torso between the thorax (chest) and pelvis in humans and in other vertebrates.
treatment_summary: Treatment may involve surgery to repair damaged organs or blood vessels. Supportive care, such as pain management and fluid resuscitation, is also important.

description: Arm wrestling (also spelled "armwrestling") is a sport in which two participants, facing each other with their bent elbows placed on a flat surface (usually a table) and hands firmly gripped, each attempt to "pin" their opponent's hand by forcing it to the surface.
treatment_summary: Treatment options range from rest and immobilization to physical therapy and, in some cases, surgery. Pain management is also a key component of care.

description: In medicine, an avulsion is an injury in which a body structure is torn off by either trauma or surgery.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on repairing damaged tissues and restoring function and appearance. Surgery may be required for more severe injuries.

description: A mallet finger, also known as hammer finger or PLF finger or Hannan finger, is an extensor tendon injury at the farthest away finger joint.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes splinting to keep the finger straight and allow the tendon to heal. In some cases, surgery may be necessary to repair the tendon.

description: The hand is a very complex organ with multiple joints, different types of ligament, tendons and nerves.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies depending on the type and severity of the injury, but may involve splinting, physical therapy, or surgery. The goal is to restore function and reduce pain.

description: Injury Reserve was an American hip-hop group founded in Tempe, Arizona, in 2012.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery to repair or replace the hip joint. Physical therapy is essential for rehabilitation, and pain medication may be prescribed.

description: In humans and other primates, the knee joins the thigh with the leg and consists of two joints: one between the femur and tibia (tibiofemoral joint), and one between the femur and patella (patellofemoral joint).
treatment_summary: Treatment ranges from physical therapy to surgery depending on severity. Pain medication and supportive care can help manage symptoms.

description: Injury Reserve was an American hip-hop group founded in Tempe, Arizona, in 2012.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes physical therapy to restore function and surgery for severe injuries. Medications address pain, while supportive care aids healing.

description: Shoulder problems, including pain, are one of the more common reasons for physician visits for musculoskeletal symptoms.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes physical therapy, pain medication, and sometimes surgery. Supportive care focuses on rest and activity modification.

description: A spinal cord injury (SCI) is damage to the spinal cord that causes temporary or permanent changes in its function.
treatment_summary: Treatment often requires surgery to stabilize the spine, along with extensive physical therapy. Medications and supportive care manage pain and prevent complications.

description: Sun scald is the freezing of bark following high temperatures in the winter season, resulting in permanent visible damage to bark.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on pain management and supporting the healing process. Physical therapy helps to restore strength and mobility.

description: Many species of arthropods (insects, arachnids, millipedes and centipedes) can bite or sting human beings.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on relieving symptoms like itching and swelling. Topical or oral medications may be used to treat allergic reactions or prevent infection.

related_diseases.disease: envenomation from spider or animal bite

description: Claus von Bülow (born Claus Cecil Borberg; 11 August 1926 – 25 May 2019) was a British lawyer, consultant and socialite.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves supportive care to stabilize blood sugar levels. Monitoring is critical to prevent complications.

description: Interstitial lung disease (ILD), or diffuse parenchymal lung disease (DPLD), is a group of respiratory diseases affecting the interstitium (the tissue) and space around the alveoli (air sacs) of the lungs.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves medication to suppress the immune system and slow disease progression. Supportive care, including oxygen therapy and pulmonary rehabilitation, can improve quality of life.

description: Intertrigo, commonly called "skin fold dermatitis", refers to a type of inflammatory rash (dermatitis) of the superficial skin that occurs within a person's body folds.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes topical medications to reduce inflammation and infection. Lifestyle changes such as weight loss and improved hygiene are important.

description: Colorectal cancer, also known as bowel cancer, colon cancer, or rectal cancer, is the development of cancer from the colon or rectum (parts of the large intestine).
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery to remove the tumor, followed by chemotherapy and radiation therapy. Palliative care addresses symptoms and improves quality of life.

description: The gastrointestinal tract (also called the GI tract, digestive tract, and the alimentary canal) is the tract or passageway of the digestive system that leads from the mouth to the anus.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the specific intestinal disease but may include medication to control symptoms, lifestyle changes to improve gut health, or surgery to correct structural problems.

description: Malabsorption is a state arising from abnormality in absorption of food nutrients across the gastrointestinal (GI) tract.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on addressing the underlying cause of malabsorption and managing symptoms. Dietary modifications and nutritional supplements are often necessary.

description: Bowel obstruction, also known as intestinal obstruction, is a mechanical or functional obstruction of the intestines that prevents the normal movement of the products of digestion.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery to remove the obstruction. Supportive care, such as IV fluids and bowel rest, is also crucial.

description: Intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH), also known as hemorrhagic stroke, is a sudden bleeding into the tissues of the brain (i.e.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on controlling bleeding and reducing pressure on the brain. This may include surgery, medication, and supportive care.

description: Brain abscess (or cerebral abscess) is an abscess within the brain tissue caused by inflammation and collection of infected material coming from local (ear infection, dental abscess, infection of paranasal sinuses, infection of the mastoid air cells of the temporal bone, epidural abscess) or remote (lung, heart, kidney etc.) infectious sources.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves draining the abscess surgically and administering antibiotics to eradicate the infection. Long-term follow-up is essential.

description: Intracranial hemorrhage (ICH) refers to any form of bleeding within the skull.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the cause and location of the hemorrhage, but may include surgery to relieve pressure or medications to control bleeding. Supportive care is crucial.

description:
Intussusception may refer to:

Intussusception (medical disorder)
Intussusception (blood vessel growth)
Rectal prolapse
Internal rectal prolapse
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery to reduce the intussusception. In some cases, a barium enema may be used to non-surgically correct the problem.

description: Uveitis () is inflammation of the uvea, the pigmented layer of the eye between the inner retina and the outer fibrous layer composed of the sclera and cornea.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing inflammation with eye drops and oral medications. Addressing any underlying causes is also important.

description: Iron-deficiency anemia is anemia caused by a lack of iron.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves iron supplements to replenish iron stores. Dietary changes to increase iron intake are also recommended.

description: Irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) is a functional gastrointestinal disorder characterized by a group of symptoms that commonly include abdominal pain, abdominal bloating, and changes in the consistency of bowel movements.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms through diet, lifestyle changes, medications, and behavioral therapies. There is no cure, but symptoms can be managed.

description: Intestinal ischemia is a medical condition in which injury to the large or small intestine occurs due to inadequate blood supply.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on restoring blood flow to the bowel. This may involve surgery to remove blockages or damaged tissue, as well as medications to prevent blood clots.

description: Coronary artery disease (CAD), also called coronary heart disease (CHD), or ischemic heart disease (IHD), is a type of heart disease involving the reduction of blood flow to the cardiac muscle due to a build-up of atheromatous plaque in the arteries of the heart.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to improve blood flow to the heart. This can involve medications to lower cholesterol and blood pressure, surgery to bypass blocked arteries, and lifestyle changes like diet and exercise.

description: An itch (also known as pruritus) is a sensation that causes a strong desire or reflex to scratch.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on relieving the itch. This can involve topical creams, oral medications, and avoiding irritants.

description: Prognathism is a positional relationship of the mandible or maxilla to the skeletal base where either of the jaws protrudes beyond a predetermined imaginary line in the coronal plane of the skull.
treatment_summary: Treatment may include surgery to reposition the jaw. Other options are physical therapy and pain medication.

description: A joint effusion is the presence of increased intra-articular fluid.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce swelling and pain in the joint. This can involve pain medication, physical therapy, and draining the fluid from the joint.

description: Juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA), formerly known as juvenile rheumatoid arthritis (JRA), is the most common chronic rheumatic disease of childhood, affecting approximately 3.8 to 400 out of 100,000 children.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to manage inflammation and pain. This may involve medications, physical therapy, and lifestyle adjustments.

description: Kaposi's sarcoma (KS) is a type of cancer that can form masses on the skin, in lymph nodes, in the mouth, or in other organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies depending on the extent and location of the disease. It may involve surgery, radiation therapy, chemotherapy, or medications to boost the immune system.

description: Kidney cancer, also known as renal cancer, is a group of cancers that starts in the kidney.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery to remove the tumor. Chemotherapy, radiation, and targeted therapies may also be used, as well as supportive care to manage symptoms.

disease: kidney disease due to longstanding hypertension
description: Idiopathic intracranial hypertension (IIH), previously known as pseudotumor cerebri and benign intracranial hypertension, is a condition characterized by increased intracranial pressure (pressure around the brain) without a detectable cause.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing blood pressure and protecting kidney function. This includes medications, lifestyle changes such as diet and exercise, and regular monitoring of kidney function.

description: Kidney failure, also known as renal failure or end-stage renal disease (ESRD), is a medical condition in which the kidneys can no longer adequately filter waste products from the blood, functioning at less than 15% of normal levels.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to manage symptoms and slow the progression of kidney failure. This involves medications, lifestyle changes, dialysis, and kidney transplant.

description: Kidney stone disease or urinary stone disease is a crystallopathy that occurs when there is an excess of minerals in the urine and not enough liquid or hydration.

risk_factors.label: Diet high in protein, sodium, and sugar

treatment_summary: Treatment involves pain management, increasing fluid intake, and sometimes medication to help pass the stone. Larger stones may require surgical removal.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: A tear of a meniscus is a rupturing of one or more of the fibrocartilage strips in the knee called menisci.
treatment_summary: Treatment options range from physical therapy and bracing to surgical repair, depending on the severity and location of the tear. Pain medication may also be used.

description: Labyrinthitis is inflammation of the labyrinth, a maze of fluid-filled channels in the inner ear.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms like vertigo and nausea, often with medication. Physical therapy can help with balance issues.

description: Lactose intolerance is caused by a lessened ability or a complete inability to digest lactose, a sugar found in dairy products.
treatment_summary: Management involves avoiding lactose-containing foods or using lactase enzyme supplements. Dietary adjustments can minimize symptoms.

description: Laryngitis is inflammation of the larynx (voice box).
treatment_summary: Treatment includes resting the voice, increasing fluid intake, and using a humidifier. Medications may be prescribed for bacterial infections or to reduce inflammation.

description: Tennis elbow, also known as lateral epicondylitis, is an enthesopathy (attachment point disease) of the origin of the extensor carpi radialis brevis on the lateral epicondyle.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing pain and inflammation through physical therapy, pain relievers, and sometimes steroid injections. Rest and activity modification are important.

description: Leukemia (also spelled leukaemia; pronounced  loo-KEE-mee-ə) is a group of blood cancers that usually begin in the bone marrow and produce high numbers of abnormal blood cells.
treatment_summary: Treatment often includes chemotherapy, radiation therapy, and stem cell transplantation. Supportive care is crucial to manage side effects.

description:
Lewy body dementia (LBD) is two similar and common subtypes of dementia: dementia with Lewy bodies (DLB) and
Parkinson's disease dementia (PDD). Both are characterized by changes in thinking, movement, behavior, and mood. The two conditions have similar features and may have similar causes, and are believed to belong on a spectrum of Lewy body disease that includes Parkinson's disease.  As of 2014, they were more often misdiagnosed than any other common dementia.
The exact cause is unknown, but involves widespread deposits of abnormal clumps of protein that form in neurons of the diseased brain. Known as Lewy bodies (discovered in 1912 by Frederic Lewy) and Lewy neurites, these clumps affect both the central nervous system and the autonomic nervous system. The fifth revision of the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM-5) gives Lewy body disease as the causative subtype of dementia with Lewy bodies, and Parkinson's disease as the causative subtype of Parkinson's disease dementia. Dementia with Lewy bodies is marked by the presence of Lewy bodies primarily in the cortical regions, and Parkinson's disease dementia with Lewy bodies primarily in the subcortical basal ganglia.
treatment_summary: There is no cure; treatment focuses on managing cognitive and motor symptoms. Medications and behavioral therapies can improve quality of life.

description: Louse (pl.: lice) is the common name for any member of the infraorder Phthiraptera, which contains nearly 5,000 species of wingless parasitic insects.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves using medicated shampoos or lotions to kill the lice and nits. Thorough cleaning of personal items is also necessary.

description: Lichen planus (LP) is a chronic inflammatory and autoimmune disease that affects the skin, nails, hair, and mucous membranes.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce itching and inflammation with topical or oral corticosteroids. Antihistamines may also be used to relieve itching.

description: Lichen simplex chronicus (LSC) is thick leathery skin with exaggerated skin markings caused by sudden itching and excessive rubbing and scratching.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves topical corticosteroids to reduce inflammation and behavioral therapy to stop scratching. Emollients are also helpful to restore the skin.

description: A lipoma is a benign tumor made of fat tissue.
treatment_summary: Lipomas are usually harmless and may not require treatment. Surgical removal is an option if the lipoma is painful or growing.

description: Liver cancer, also known as hepatic cancer, primary hepatic cancer, or primary hepatic malignancy, is cancer that starts in the liver.

risk_factors.label: Chronic hepatitis B or C infection

treatment_summary: Treatment options include surgery, liver transplantation, chemotherapy, radiation therapy, targeted drug therapy, and immunotherapy. Palliative care can help manage symptoms and improve quality of life.

description: Liver disease, or hepatic disease, is any of many diseases of the liver.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the specific liver disease, and includes lifestyle changes such as diet and exercise, medications to manage the underlying cause, and in severe cases, liver transplantation. Supportive care focuses on managing symptoms and complications.

description: Low back pain or lumbago is a common disorder involving the muscles, nerves, and bones of the back, in between the lower edge of the ribs and the lower fold of the buttocks.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes physical therapy, pain relievers, and lifestyle modifications such as exercise and proper posture. In some cases, stronger medications or surgery may be necessary.

description: Lung cancer, also called lung carcinoma, is a malignant tumor that originates in the tissues of the lungs.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include surgery, chemotherapy, radiation therapy, targeted therapy, and immunotherapy. Palliative care helps manage symptoms and improve quality of life.

description: A pulmonary contusion, also known as a lung contusion, is a bruise of the lung, caused by chest trauma.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on supportive care, including pain management, oxygen therapy, and monitoring for complications. In severe cases, mechanical ventilation may be required.

description: Lyme disease, also known as Lyme borreliosis, is a tick-borne disease caused by species of Borrelia bacteria, transmitted by blood-feeding ticks in the genus Ixodes.
treatment_summary: Lyme disease is treated with oral antibiotics. Early treatment can prevent long-term complications.

description: Lymphadenopathy or adenopathy is a disease of the lymph nodes, in which they are abnormal in size or consistency.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves addressing the underlying cause of the infection or inflammation, often with antibiotics or antiviral medications. Supportive care includes pain management and rest.

description: Lymphangitis is an inflammation or an infection of the lymphatic channels that occurs as a result of infection at a site distal to the channel.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves antibiotics to combat the infection. Supportive care includes elevation of the affected limb and pain management.

description: Lymphedema (USA), lymphœdema (UK) also known as lymphatic edema (US)/œdema (UK), is a condition of localized swelling caused by a compromised lymphatic system.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing swelling and managing pain through physical therapy, compression, and sometimes surgery.  Lifestyle changes can also help manage the condition.

description: Lymphogranuloma venereum (LGV; also known as climatic bubo, Durand–Nicolas–Favre disease, poradenitis inguinale, lymphogranuloma inguinale, and strumous bubo) is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the invasive serovars L1, L2, L2a, L2b, or L3 of Chlamydia trachomatis.
treatment_summary: The primary treatment is antibiotics to eradicate the Chlamydia trachomatis infection.  Early treatment can prevent long-term complications.

description: Lymphoma is a group of blood and lymph tumors that develop from lymphocytes (a type of white blood cell).
treatment_summary: Treatment varies widely depending on the type and stage of lymphoma, but commonly involves chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or immunotherapy. In some cases, surgery or stem cell transplants may be necessary.

description: Macular degeneration, also known as age-related macular degeneration (AMD or ARMD), is a medical condition which may result in blurred or no vision in the center of the visual field.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include medications to slow the progression of wet AMD and lifestyle changes, such as quitting smoking and adopting a healthy diet.  Surgery may be indicated in some cases.

description: Magnesium deficiency is an electrolyte disturbance in which there is a low level of magnesium in the body.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves magnesium supplementation, either orally or intravenously in severe cases. Dietary changes to increase magnesium intake are also recommended.

description: Malaria is a mosquito-borne infectious disease which is transmitted by the bite of Anopheles mosquitoes.

risk_factors.label: Living in or traveling to endemic areas

treatment_summary: Malaria is treated with antimalarial drugs, the specific drug and duration depend on the type of malaria, its severity, and drug-resistance patterns. Supportive care is also important to manage symptoms.

description: The human female reproductive system is made up of the internal and external sex organs that function in the reproduction of new offspring.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the specific infection but may include antibiotics, antifungals, or antiviral medications.  Maintaining good hygiene is also important for prevention and treatment.

description: A hypertensive emergency is very high blood pressure with potentially life-threatening symptoms and signs of acute damage to one or more organ systems (especially brain, eyes, heart, aorta, or kidneys).
treatment_summary: Malignant hypertension requires immediate treatment with intravenous antihypertensive medications to rapidly lower blood pressure. Patients also receive supportive care for organ damage.

description: Cannabis use disorder (CUD), also known as cannabis addiction or marijuana addiction, is a psychiatric disorder defined in the fifth revision of the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM-5) and ICD-11 as the continued use of cannabis despite clinically significant impairment.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves behavioral therapy, such as cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT), to address underlying issues and develop coping mechanisms.  Medication and supportive care may also be necessary.

description: Mastectomy is the medical term for the surgical removal of one or both breasts, partially or completely.
treatment_summary: A mastectomy involves surgical removal of breast tissue, usually to treat or prevent breast cancer.  Physical therapy and supportive care are important aspects of recovery.

description: Mastoiditis is the result of an infection that extends to the air cells of the skull behind the ear.
treatment_summary: Antibiotics are used to treat the infection. Surgery may be required to drain the infected area or remove damaged tissue.

description: A Meckel's diverticulum, a true congenital diverticulum, is a slight bulge in the small intestine present at birth and a vestigial remnant of the vitelline duct.
treatment_summary: Surgery is typically required to remove the diverticulum if it causes symptoms or complications. This prevents further issues.

description: Melanoma is a type of cancer, typically skin cancer; it develops from the  melanin-producing cells known as melanocytes.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes surgical removal of the melanoma. Additional therapies like medication or radiation may be needed for advanced cases.

description: Ménière's disease (MD) is a disease of the inner ear characterized by potentially severe and incapacitating episodes of vertigo, tinnitus, hearing loss, and a feeling of fullness in the ear.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to manage symptoms through medication, physical therapy, and lifestyle adjustments. Dietary changes can help reduce fluid retention.

description: Meningioma, also known as meningeal tumor, is typically a slow-growing tumor that forms from the meninges, the membranous layers surrounding the brain and spinal cord.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include monitoring for slow-growing tumors or surgical removal when necessary. Radiation therapy may be used in some cases.

description: Meningitis (pl.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves antibiotics or antiviral medications, depending on the cause. Supportive care is essential for managing symptoms and preventing complications.

description: Menopause is the time when menstrual periods permanently stop, marking the end of the reproductive stage for the female human.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms through hormone therapy or lifestyle changes. These help alleviate discomfort.

description: A metabolic disorder is a disorder that negatively alters the body's processing and distribution of macronutrients, such as proteins, fats, and carbohydrates.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves managing the specific metabolic issue, often through diet, exercise, and medication. The goal is to restore metabolic balance.

description: Metastasis is the spread of a pathogenic agent from an initial or primary site to a different or secondary site within the host's body; the term is typically used when referring to metastasis by a cancerous tumor.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to control the spread of cancer and manage symptoms. Options include systemic therapies like chemotherapy or targeted therapy and palliative care to improve quality of life.

description: Migraine (UK: , US: ) is a neurological disorder characterized by episodes of moderate-to-severe headache, most often unilateral and generally associated with nausea, light sensitivity and sound sensitivity.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes medications to relieve pain and prevent future migraines, along with lifestyle changes. Identifying and avoiding triggers can help manage migraines.

description: Miscarriage, also known in medical terms as a spontaneous abortion, is an end to pregnancy resulting in the loss and expulsion of an embryo or fetus from the womb before it can survive independently.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on the removal of the pregnancy tissue. This can be done through medication, surgery (D&C), or allowing the process to occur naturally with supportive care.

description: Mitral regurgitation (MR), also known as mitral insufficiency or mitral incompetence, is a form of valvular heart disease in which the mitral valve is insufficient and does not close properly when the heart pumps out blood.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes medications to manage symptoms and prevent complications. Severe cases may require surgical repair or replacement of the mitral valve.

description: Mittelschmerz (German: [ˈmɪtl̩ʃmɛʁt͡s] ) is a term for pain due to ovulation.
treatment_summary: Treatment usually involves over-the-counter pain relievers and heat application. Symptoms are typically mild and self-limiting.

description: Molluscum contagiosum (MC), sometimes called water warts, is a viral infection of the skin that results in small raised pink lesions with a dimple in the center.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include topical creams, minor surgical procedures to remove the lesions, or allowing the infection to resolve on its own. In many cases, no treatment is needed as the infection will clear up eventually.

description: Peripheral neuropathy, often shortened to neuropathy, refers to damage or disease affecting the nerves.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on addressing the underlying cause, pain management, and physical therapy to improve function. Supportive care may involve braces or splints.

description: Infectious mononucleosis (IM, mono), also known as glandular fever, is an infection usually caused by the Epstein–Barr virus (EBV).
treatment_summary: Treatment is primarily supportive, focusing on rest, hydration, and pain relief. Corticosteroids may be used in severe cases.

description: Moyamoya disease is a disease in which certain arteries in the brain are constricted.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on preventing strokes and improving blood flow to the brain, often through surgical revascularization procedures. Medications can help manage symptoms.

description: Mucositis is the painful inflammation and ulceration of the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, usually as an adverse effect of  chemotherapy and radiotherapy treatment for cancer.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes pain management, good oral hygiene, and medications to coat and protect the mucous membranes. Nutritional support is also important.

description: Multiple myeloma (MM), also known as plasma cell myeloma and simply myeloma, is a cancer of plasma cells, a type of white blood cell that normally produces antibodies.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves chemotherapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy, and stem cell transplantation. Supportive care and physical therapy are also crucial.

related_diseases.disease: anemia due to chronic kidney disease

description: Multiple sclerosis (MS) is an autoimmune disease resulting in damage to myelin, which are the insulating covers of nerve cells in the brain and spinal cord.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms, slowing disease progression, and improving quality of life. This includes medications, physical therapy, and lifestyle modifications.

description: Mumps is a highly contagious, vaccine-preventable viral disease caused by the mumps virus.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on alleviating symptoms and providing supportive care until the infection resolves. Rest, fluids, and pain management are typical.

description: A spasm is a sudden involuntary contraction of a muscle, a group of muscles, or a hollow organ, such as the bladder.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves addressing the underlying cause, such as dehydration or electrolyte imbalance. Medications and physical therapy can help relieve spasm and improve muscle function.

description: Muscular dystrophy (MD) is a genetically and clinically heterogeneous group of rare neuromuscular diseases that cause progressive weakness and breakdown of skeletal muscles over time.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and improving quality of life, as there is no cure. Physical therapy, medications, and supportive care are common approaches.

description: Myasthenia gravis (MG) is a long-term neuromuscular junction disease that leads to varying degrees of skeletal muscle weakness.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to improve muscle weakness and control symptoms. Medications, thymectomy (surgical removal of the thymus gland), and supportive therapies are utilized.

related_diseases.disease: anemia due to chronic kidney disease

description: A myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) is one of a group of cancers in which blood cells in the bone marrow do not mature, and as a result, do not develop into healthy blood cells.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies depending on the severity and risk factors, including blood transfusions, medications to stimulate blood cell production, chemotherapy, or stem cell transplant. Supportive care helps manage complications.

related_diseases.disease: kidney disease due to longstanding hypertension

description: Myocarditis is inflammation of the cardiac muscle.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on addressing the underlying cause and reducing inflammation. Supportive care and medications to manage heart failure or arrhythmias may be necessary.

description: Myoclonus is a brief, involuntary, irregular (lacking rhythm) twitching  of a muscle, a joint, or a group of muscles, different from clonus, which is rhythmic or regular.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the cause and severity. Medications to control seizures or muscle spasms, as well as behavioral therapies, may be used.

description: Myopia, also known as near-sightedness and short-sightedness, is an eye condition where light from distant objects focuses in front of, instead of on, the retina.
treatment_summary: Myopia is corrected with corrective lenses or refractive surgery such as LASIK. Atropine eye drops can sometimes slow the progression.

related_diseases.disease: retinopathy due to high blood pressure
central retinal artery or vein occlusion

description: Myositis is a rarely-encountered medical condition characterized by inflammation affecting the muscles.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the cause. Corticosteroids and immunosuppressants may be used to reduce inflammation, along with physical therapy to maintain muscle strength.

description: Narcolepsy is a chronic neurological disorder that impairs the ability to regulate sleep–wake cycles, and specifically impacts REM (rapid eye movement) sleep.
