treatment_summary: Treatment involves medications to manage daytime sleepiness and cataplexy. Behavioral therapies and lifestyle adjustments, such as scheduled naps, can also help.

description: Nasal polyps are noncancerous growths within the nose or sinuses.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include corticosteroids to reduce inflammation and surgery to remove the polyps.  Saline nasal sprays can also help manage symptoms.

description: Necrotizing fasciitis (NF), also known as flesh-eating disease, is an infection that kills the body's soft tissue.
treatment_summary: Immediate treatment includes strong antibiotics and surgical removal of infected tissue. Supportive care is critical for recovery.

description: Neonatal jaundice is a yellowish discoloration of the white part of the eyes and skin in a newborn baby due to high bilirubin levels.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves phototherapy to help break down bilirubin.  In some cases, exchange transfusions are necessary.

description: Radiculopathy (from Latin  radix 'root'; from Ancient Greek  πάθος (pathos) 'suffering'), also commonly referred to as pinched nerve, refers to a set of conditions in which one or more nerves are affected and do not work properly (a neuropathy).
treatment_summary: Treatment includes physical therapy to strengthen muscles and reduce nerve compression. Surgery may be needed in severe cases.

description: Neuralgia (Greek neuron, "nerve" + algos, "pain") is pain in the distribution of a nerve or nerves, as in intercostal neuralgia, trigeminal neuralgia, and glossopharyngeal neuralgia.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on pain management with medications and nerve blocks. Surgery might be considered if other treatments fail.

description: Neurofibromatosis (NF) refers to a group of three distinct genetic conditions in which tumors grow in the nervous system.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves monitoring tumor growth and managing symptoms. Surgery or medication may be required for larger tumors.

description: Peripheral neuropathy, often shortened to neuropathy, refers to damage or disease affecting the nerves.
treatment_summary: Management includes pain relief and physical therapy.  Adjusting or discontinuing the causative drug may be necessary.

description: Neurosis (pl.
treatment_summary: Psychotherapy can help patients understand and manage symptoms. Medications can be used to treat co-occurring conditions such as anxiety or depression.

description: Fatty liver disease (FLD), also known as hepatic steatosis and steatotic liver disease (SLD), is a condition where excess fat builds up in the liver.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on lifestyle changes like diet and exercise.  Medications may be used to manage related conditions like diabetes and high cholesterol.

description: Gastroenteritis, also known as infectious diarrhea, is an inflammation of the gastrointestinal tract including the stomach and intestine.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves managing symptoms with hydration and medications. Avoiding trigger foods can also help.

description: Normal pressure hydrocephalus (NPH), also called malresorptive hydrocephalus, is a form of communicating hydrocephalus in which excess cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) builds up in the ventricles, leading to normal or slightly elevated cerebrospinal fluid pressure.
treatment_summary: Surgical shunting can help drain excess cerebrospinal fluid. Supportive care addresses associated symptoms.

description: Nasal septum deviation is a physical disorder of the nose, involving a displacement of the nasal septum.
treatment_summary: Surgery can correct nasal septum deviation. Medications may treat related symptoms.

description: Obesity is a medical condition, considered by multiple organizations to be a disease, in which excess body fat has accumulated to such an extent that it can have negative effects on health.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes lifestyle changes, medications, and surgery. Behavioral therapy can address eating habits.

description: Obsessive–compulsive disorder (OCD) is a mental disorder in which an individual has intrusive thoughts (an obsession) and feels the need to perform certain behaviors (compulsions) repeatedly to relieve the distress caused by the obsession, to the extent where it impairs general function.
treatment_summary: Behavioral therapy and medication are used to manage OCD. Therapy helps change thought patterns and behaviors.

description: Obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) is the most common sleep-related breathing disorder.
treatment_summary: Treatments include lifestyle changes, oral appliances, CPAP, and surgery. The goal is to maintain an open airway during sleep.

description: Omphalitis of newborn is the medical term for inflammation of the umbilical cord stump in the neonatal newborn period, most commonly attributed to a bacterial infection.
treatment_summary: Antibiotics are used to treat the bacterial infection. Supportive care includes cleaning and monitoring the area.

description: Onychomycosis, also known as tinea unguium, is a fungal infection of the nail.
treatment_summary: Oral and topical antifungal medications are used. Treatment duration can be long.

description: Penetrating trauma is an open wound injury that occurs when an object pierces the skin and enters a tissue of the body, creating a deep but relatively narrow entry wound.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves cleaning, closing the wound, and preventing infection. Surgery may be needed for complex wounds.

description: A surgical incision is a cut made through the skin and soft tissue to facilitate an operation or procedure.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves keeping the incision clean and preventing infection. Topical medications can aid healing.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves controlling bleeding and preventing infection. Surgery may be required to repair internal damage.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves cleaning the wound, controlling bleeding, and preventing infection. More severe wounds may require stitches or surgery.

description: Penetrating trauma is an open wound injury that occurs when an object pierces the skin and enters a tissue of the body, creating a deep but relatively narrow entry wound.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on stabilizing the patient, cleaning and closing the wound, and preventing infection. Surgery may be required to repair damaged tissues.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves cleaning the wound, controlling bleeding, and preventing infection. Facial wounds often require careful closure to minimize scarring.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment prioritizes stabilizing the patient, addressing breathing difficulties, and preventing infection. Surgery is often needed to repair damage to internal organs.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to clean the wound, prevent infection, and restore the ear's structure. Surgery may be needed to repair torn cartilage or skin.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on protecting the eye, preventing infection, and preserving vision. Surgical repair may be needed for severe injuries.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves cleaning the wound, controlling bleeding, and minimizing scarring. Surgical repair is often required to restore facial appearance.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes cleaning the wound, controlling bleeding, and preventing infection. Surgery may be necessary for deep cuts or fractures.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to clean the wound, prevent infection, and promote healing. Diabetics require specialized care to avoid complications.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves cleaning the wound, controlling bleeding, and preserving hand function. Physical therapy may be needed to regain full use.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves cleaning and closing the wound. Antibiotics may be used to prevent infection.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on cleaning the wound, controlling bleeding, and preventing infection. Surgery may be needed for severe injuries.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes cleaning, suturing, and possibly antibiotics. Physical therapy may be needed for rehabilitation.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: The lip wound should be cleaned and closed. Sutures may be needed for deep cuts.

description: Wound licking is an instinctive response in humans and many other animals to cover an injury or second degree burn with saliva.
treatment_summary: Treatment consists of cleaning the wound and preventing infection. Salt water rinses and antiseptic mouthwash are commonly used.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment may require immediate medical attention to control bleeding and prevent infection. Surgery may be necessary for repair.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves stopping the bleeding and cleaning the wound. Topical antibiotics may be used.

description: A wound is any disruption of or damage to living tissue, such as skin, mucous membranes, or organs.
treatment_summary: The wound should be cleaned and may require sutures. Physical therapy might be needed to regain shoulder function.

description: Oppositional defiant disorder (ODD) is listed in the DSM-5 under Disruptive, impulse-control, and conduct disorders and defined as "a pattern of angry/irritable mood, argumentative/defiant behavior, or vindictiveness." This behavior is usually targeted toward peers, parents, teachers, and other authority figures, including law enforcement officials.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes therapy to help the child and family develop coping strategies. Medications may be prescribed to manage co-existing conditions.

description: Optic neuritis (ON) is a debilitating condition that is defined as inflammation of cranial nerve II which results in disruption of the neurologic pathways that allow visual sensory information received by the retina to be able to be transmitted to the visual cortex of the brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves corticosteroids to reduce inflammation. Monitoring for underlying conditions like multiple sclerosis is important.

related_diseases.disease: central retinal artery or vein occlusion

description: Oral leukoplakia is a potentially malignant disorder affecting the oral mucosa.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves surgical removal of the lesion and topical medications. Regular monitoring is necessary to detect any malignant transformation.

description: Aphthous stomatitis, or recurrent aphthous stomatitis (RAS), commonly referred to as a canker sore or salt blister, is a common condition characterized by the repeated formation of benign and non-contagious mouth ulcers (aphthae) in otherwise healthy individuals.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on topical applications to reduce pain and inflammation. Supportive care involves maintaining good oral hygiene and avoiding irritants.

description: Oral candidiasis (Acute pseudomembranous candidiasis), also known among other names as oral thrush, is candidiasis that occurs in the mouth.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves oral or topical antifungal medications. Maintaining good oral hygiene is also important.

description: Orbital cellulitis  is inflammation of eye tissues behind the orbital septum.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antibiotics, often administered intravenously. Surgery may be necessary in severe cases to drain abscesses.

description: Orthostatic hypotension, also known as postural hypotension or commonly known as headrush, is a medical condition wherein a person's blood pressure drops (hypotension) when they are standing up (orthostasis) or sitting down.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes lifestyle modifications like staying hydrated and medication adjustments. Supportive care may involve wearing compression stockings.

description: Osteoarthritis is a type of degenerative joint disease that results from breakdown of joint cartilage and underlying bone.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on pain management through medication and physical therapy. Lifestyle changes, such as weight management, and surgery are other options.

description: Osteochondroma is the most common benign tumor of bone.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves monitoring for growth and symptoms. Surgical removal is considered if the tumor causes pain or interferes with movement.

description: Osteochondrosis is a family of orthopedic diseases of the joint that occur in children, adolescents and rapidly growing animals, particularly pigs, horses, dogs, and broiler chickens.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on physical therapy to improve joint function. Lifestyle changes, such as activity modification, and surgery may also be needed.

description: Osteomyelitis (OM) is the infectious inflammation of bone.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically requires antibiotics, often administered intravenously for several weeks. Surgery may be necessary to remove infected bone tissue.

description: Osteoporosis is a systemic skeletal disorder characterized by low bone mass (osteopenia), micro-architectural deterioration of bone tissue leading to more porous bone, and consequent increase in fracture risk.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves medications to increase bone density and reduce fracture risk. Lifestyle changes such as weight-bearing exercise and adequate calcium intake are also important.

description: Otitis externa, also called swimmer's ear, is inflammation of the ear canal.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves ear drops to combat infection and reduce inflammation. Keeping the ear dry and avoiding irritants is also important.

description: Otitis media is a group of inflammatory diseases of the middle ear.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves antibiotics to clear the infection.  Pain management and observation may be used for mild cases.

description: Otosclerosis is a condition of the middle and inner ear where portions of the dense enchondral layer of the bony labyrinth remodel into one or more lesions of irregularly-laid spongy bone.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes hearing aids to improve hearing or surgery (stapedectomy) to restore hearing. Monitoring hearing loss progression is also an option.

description: Ovarian cancer is a cancerous tumor of an ovary.

risk_factors.label: Family history of ovarian or breast cancer

treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves surgery to remove the tumor followed by chemotherapy. Targeted therapy may also be used based on the cancer's characteristics.

description: An ovarian cyst is a fluid-filled sac within the ovary.
treatment_summary: Many ovarian cysts resolve on their own with monitoring.  Oral contraceptives may be used to prevent new cysts, and surgery may be needed for large or symptomatic cysts.

description: Ovarian torsion (OT) or adnexal torsion is an abnormal condition where an ovary twists on its attachment to other structures, such that blood flow is decreased.
treatment_summary: Surgery is required to untwist the ovary and restore blood flow, or to remove the ovary if it is no longer viable.

description: Overflow incontinence is a concept of urinary incontinence, characterized by the involuntary release of urine from an overfull urinary bladder, often in the absence of any urge to urinate.
treatment_summary: Treatment addresses the underlying cause, such as medications to improve bladder emptying or catheterization. Lifestyle changes may also help.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: Pain Hustlers is a 2023 American black comedy crime film co-produced and directed by David Yates from a screenplay by Wells Tower, based on the 2022 book of the same name by Evan Hughes.
treatment_summary: Pain is typically managed with oral pain medications. Physical therapy can help restore function and reduce discomfort.

description: Rheumatism  (from the Ancient Greek ῥεῦμα, rheûma) or rheumatic disorders are conditions causing chronic, often intermittent pain affecting the joints or connective tissue.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on pain relief, improving function, and preventing recurrence. This often includes pain medication, physical therapy, and lifestyle changes.

description: Pancreatic cancer is any cancer with a primary location in the pancreas, a glandular organ lying behind the stomach.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include surgery to remove the tumor, chemotherapy, and radiation therapy. Palliative care focuses on managing symptoms and improving quality of life.

description: Panic attacks are sudden periods of intense fear and discomfort that may include palpitations, otherwise defined as a rapid, irregular heartbeat, sweating, chest pain or discomfort, shortness of breath, trembling, dizziness, numbness, confusion, or a sense of impending doom or loss of control.
treatment_summary: Treatment often includes cognitive behavioral therapy to address thought patterns and medication to manage symptoms. Lifestyle adjustments such as regular exercise and relaxation techniques can also help.

description: Panic disorder is an anxiety disorder characterized by reoccurring unexpected panic attacks.

risk_factors.label: temperament that is sensitive to stress

treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves a combination of cognitive behavioral therapy and medication, such as antidepressants or anti-anxiety drugs. The goal is to reduce the frequency and intensity of panic attacks.

description: A parasitic disease, also known as parasitosis, is an infectious disease caused by parasites.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antiparasitic medications specific to the type of parasite. Hygiene and sanitation improvements are also crucial for prevention.

description: A parathyroid adenoma is a benign tumor of the parathyroid gland.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves surgical removal of the adenoma. In some cases, monitoring may be recommended if the adenoma is small and not causing significant symptoms.

description: Parkinson's disease (PD), or simply Parkinson's, is a neurodegenerative disease primarily of the central nervous system, affecting both motor and non-motor systems.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes medications to manage symptoms, physical therapy to improve mobility, and sometimes surgery for advanced cases. Supportive care and lifestyle adjustments are also important.

description: Paronychia is an inflammation of the skin around the nail, often due to bacteria or fungi.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves topical or oral antibiotics or antifungals, depending on the cause. In some cases, draining the infected area may be necessary.

description: Supraventricular tachycardia (SVT) is an umbrella term for fast heart rhythms arising from the upper part of the heart.
treatment_summary: Treatment can include vagal maneuvers, medications to slow the heart rate, or catheter ablation. Lifestyle modifications can help prevent episodes.

description: Ventricular tachycardia (V-tach or VT) is a cardiovascular disorder in which fast heart rate occurs in the ventricles of the heart.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to restore a normal heart rhythm and prevent future episodes. This may involve medication, cardioversion, or implantable devices.

description: Vesicovaginal fistula (VVF) is a subtype of female urogenital fistula (UGF).
treatment_summary: The primary treatment for pelvic fistula is surgical repair to close the abnormal opening. This can be done through various surgical techniques.

description: Pelvic inflammatory disease (PID), also known as pelvic inflammatory disorder, is an infection of the upper part of the female reproductive system, mainly the uterus, fallopian tubes, and ovaries, and inside of the pelvis.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antibiotics to eradicate the infection. Early treatment is crucial to prevent long-term complications.

description: Pelvic organ prolapse (POP) is characterized by descent of pelvic organs from their normal positions into the vagina.
treatment_summary: Treatment options range from pelvic floor exercises and lifestyle changes to surgical repair. The goal is to alleviate symptoms and improve quality of life.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: Pemphigus ( or ) is a rare group of blistering autoimmune diseases that affect the skin and mucous membranes.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on suppressing the immune system with medications like corticosteroids and immunosuppressants. Topical treatments can help manage skin lesions.

description: Pericarditis (PER-i-kar-DYE-tis) is inflammation of the pericardium, the fibrous sac surrounding the heart.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce inflammation and manage pain, often with NSAIDs or colchicine. In some cases, antibiotics or surgery may be needed.

description: Peripheral artery disease (PAD) is a vascular disorder that causes abnormal narrowing of arteries other than those that supply the heart or brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes lifestyle changes like quitting smoking and exercise, medications to improve blood flow, and in severe cases, surgery or angioplasty.

related_diseases.disease: kidney disease due to longstanding hypertension

description: Arterial embolism is a sudden interruption of blood flow to an organ or body part due to an embolus adhering to the wall of an artery blocking the flow of blood, the major type of embolus being a blood clot (thromboembolism).
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves medications to dissolve the clot and prevent further clotting, and in some cases, surgery to remove the blockage.

description: Cramp fasciculation syndrome (CFS) is a rare peripheral nerve hyperexcitability disorder.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and improving quality of life. Medications can help with pain and muscle spasms, while physical therapy can improve strength and mobility.

description: Group B streptococcal infection, also known as Group B streptococcal disease or just Group B strep infection, is the infectious disease caused by the bacterium Streptococcus agalactiae.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes antibiotics to fight the infection and potentially surgical drainage of any abscesses. Good hygiene practices are important for prevention.

description: Peritonitis is inflammation of the localized or generalized peritoneum, the lining of the inner wall of the abdomen and covering of the abdominal organs.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antibiotics to combat the infection and surgery to address the underlying cause. Supportive care, like fluid and electrolyte replacement, is also essential.

description: A peritonsillar abscess (PTA), also known as a quinsy, is an accumulation of pus due to an infection behind the tonsil.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes antibiotics to fight the infection and drainage of the abscess. Tonsillectomy may be considered for recurrent cases.

description: Cyclic vomiting syndrome (CVS) is a chronic functional condition of unknown pathogenesis.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and preventing episodes, often with antiemetic medications and lifestyle adjustments. Behavioral therapies may help with stress management.

description: A personality disorder (PD) is a mental disorder characterized by an enduring and pervasive maladaptive pattern of behavior, emotions, cognition, and inner experience, deviating from social norms.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves psychotherapy to address maladaptive patterns. Medications may be used to manage specific symptoms.

description: Peyronie's disease (PD) is a benign, acquired penile connective tissue disease characterized by the occurrence of fibrotic plaques within the tunica albuginea — the dense elastic covering of the corpora cavernosa.
treatment_summary: Treatment options range from oral medications to surgical intervention to correct penile curvature. Surgery is usually reserved for severe cases.

description: Pharyngitis is inflammation of the back of the throat, known as the pharynx.

risk_factors.label: Exposure to viral or bacterial pathogens

treatment_summary: Treatment typically focuses on relieving symptoms with pain relievers and staying hydrated. Antibiotics are prescribed if the cause is bacterial.

description: Phimosis (from Greek φίμωσις phimōsis 'muzzling') is a condition in which the foreskin of the penis cannot stretch to allow it to be pulled back past the glans.
treatment_summary: Treatment may involve topical steroid creams to improve foreskin flexibility. Circumcision is considered if conservative measures fail.

description: Pilonidal disease is a type of skin infection that typically occurs as a cyst between the cheeks of the buttocks and often at the upper end.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves draining the cyst and sometimes surgical removal. Antibiotics may be used to treat infection.

description: A pinguecula is a common type of conjunctival stromal degeneration in the eye.
treatment_summary: Treatment usually involves lubricating eye drops to relieve irritation. Surgery may be considered in rare cases if the pinguecula causes significant discomfort or vision problems.

description: Pinworm infection (threadworm infection in the UK), also known as enterobiasis, is a human parasitic disease caused by the pinworm, Enterobius vermicularis.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves oral medication to kill the pinworms. Strict hygiene practices are essential to prevent reinfection.

description: A pituitary adenoma is a tumor that occurs in the pituitary gland.
treatment_summary: Treatment may involve surgery to remove the tumor, medication to control hormone production, or monitoring if the tumor is small and not causing symptoms. Radiation therapy may also be an option.

description: A pituitary disease is a disorder primarily affecting the pituitary gland.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the specific disorder and may include hormone replacement therapy, medication to suppress hormone production, or surgery to remove a tumor. Monitoring is essential.

description: Pityriasis rosea is a type of skin rash.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on relieving symptoms like itching with topical corticosteroids or antihistamines. The rash typically resolves on its own within a few weeks.

description: In placenta praevia (or placenta previa), the placenta attaches inside the uterus in a position that partially or completely covers the cervical opening.
treatment_summary: Management focuses on monitoring the mother and fetus. Delivery by cesarean section is often necessary.

description: Placental abruption is when the placenta separates early from the uterus, in other words separates before childbirth.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the severity and gestational age. Delivery may be necessary, and supportive care is provided to manage complications.

description: Plantar fasciitis or plantar heel pain is a disorder of the plantar fascia, which is the connective tissue that supports the arch of the foot.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes pain relief, physical therapy, and addressing contributing factors. Orthotics and lifestyle changes can also help.

description: A pleural effusion is accumulation of excessive fluid in the pleural space, the potential space that surrounds each lung.
treatment_summary: Treatment addresses the underlying cause and may involve draining the fluid. Thoracentesis or surgery might be required.

description: Pneumoconiosis is the general term for a class of interstitial lung disease where inhalation of dust (for example, ash dust, lead particles, pollen grains etc) has caused interstitial fibrosis.
treatment_summary: There is no cure, so treatment focuses on managing symptoms and preventing further lung damage. This includes smoking cessation and avoiding further dust exposure.

description: Pneumonia is an inflammatory condition of the lung primarily affecting the small air sacs known as alveoli.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves antibiotics for bacterial pneumonia and supportive care. Hospitalization may be required for severe cases.

description: A pneumothorax is collection of air in the pleural space between the lung and the chest wall.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the size and severity. Small pneumothoraces may resolve on their own, while larger ones require chest tube insertion.

description: Salicylate poisoning, also known as aspirin poisoning, is the acute or chronic poisoning with a salicylate such as aspirin.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on supportive care, including activated charcoal to reduce absorption. Severe cases may require dialysis.

description: Barbiturate overdose is poisoning due to excessive doses of barbiturates.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes supportive care, gastric lavage, and activated charcoal to prevent absorption. Monitoring of vital signs and supportive measures are crucial.

description: Tricyclic antidepressant overdose is poisoning caused by excessive medication of the tricyclic antidepressant (TCA) type.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on supportive care, including activated charcoal and management of cardiac arrhythmias. Monitoring and symptomatic treatment are essential.

description: Antihypertensives are a class of drugs that are used to treat hypertension (high blood pressure).
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on stabilizing vital signs and preventing further absorption. Activated charcoal and specific antidotes may be administered.

description: Ciprofloxacin is a fluoroquinolone antibiotic used to treat a number of bacterial infections.
treatment_summary: Management involves supportive care, decontamination, and monitoring for complications. Specific antidotes are available for some antimicrobials.

related_diseases.disease: anemia due to chronic kidney disease

description: Antipsychotics, previously known as neuroleptics and major tranquilizers, are a class of psychotropic medication primarily used to manage psychosis (including delusions, hallucinations, paranoia or disordered thought), principally in schizophrenia but also in a range of other psychotic disorders.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes supportive care to manage symptoms and prevent complications. Activated charcoal and specific antidotes may be used in some cases.

description: Ethylene glycol poisoning is poisoning caused by drinking ethylene glycol.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves supportive care, correction of metabolic acidosis, and administration of specific antidotes such as fomepizole or ethanol. Hemodialysis may be required in severe cases.

description: Carbon monoxide poisoning typically occurs from breathing in carbon monoxide (CO) at excessive levels.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves immediate removal from the source of carbon monoxide and administration of 100% oxygen. Hyperbaric oxygen therapy may be necessary in severe cases.

description: A pesticide poisoning occurs when pesticides, chemicals intended to control a pest, affect non-target organisms such as humans, wildlife, plants, or bees.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on supporting respiration and administering naloxone, an opioid antagonist. Additional supportive measures may be necessary.

description: A sedative or tranquilizer is a substance that induces sedation by reducing irritability or excitement.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes supportive care and monitoring of vital signs. Flumazenil, a benzodiazepine antagonist, may be used in specific cases.

description: Polycystic kidney disease (PKD or PCKD, also known as polycystic kidney syndrome) is a genetic disorder in which the renal tubules become structurally abnormal, resulting in the development and growth of multiple cysts within the kidney.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and slowing disease progression. Options include medications for blood pressure control, pain management, and dialysis or kidney transplant in advanced cases.

description: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is the most common hormonal disorder in women of reproductive age.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves managing symptoms such as irregular periods, acne, and infertility. Options include hormonal birth control, medications to induce ovulation, and lifestyle modifications.

description: In oncology, polycythemia vera (PV) is an uncommon myeloproliferative neoplasm in which the bone marrow makes too many red blood cells.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing the risk of blood clots and managing symptoms. Phlebotomy, low-dose aspirin, and medications to lower blood cell counts are common interventions.

description: Polymyalgia rheumatica (PMR) is a systemic inflammatory disease characterized by pain or stiffness, usually in the neck, shoulders, upper arms, and hips, but which may occur all over the body.
treatment_summary: Corticosteroids are commonly used to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms. Dosage is tapered over time to minimize side effects.

description: Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) is a mental disorder that develops from experiencing a traumatic event, such as sexual assault, domestic violence, child abuse, warfare and its associated traumas, natural disaster, bereavement, traffic collision, or other threats on a person's life or well-being.
treatment_summary: Therapy, such as cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), helps process trauma. Medications like antidepressants can manage symptoms.

description: Pseudomonas aeruginosa is a common encapsulated, Gram-negative, aerobic–facultatively anaerobic, rod-shaped bacterium that can cause disease in plants and animals, including humans.
treatment_summary: Antibiotics are used to combat the infection, sometimes requiring intravenous administration. Surgical debridement may be necessary to remove infected tissue.

description: Postpartum depression (PPD), also known as perinatal depression, is a mood disorder which may be experienced by pregnant or postpartum women.
treatment_summary: Antidepressants can help regulate mood. Therapy, like cognitive behavioral therapy, provides coping strategies.

description: Pre-eclampsia is a multi-system disorder specific to pregnancy, characterized by the new onset of high blood pressure and often a significant amount of protein in the urine (proteinuria) or by the new onset of high blood pressure along with significant end-organ damage, with or without the proteinuria.
treatment_summary: Medications to control blood pressure are often prescribed. Delivery of the baby is the definitive treatment if the condition is severe or near term.

description: Pregnancy is the time during which one or more offspring gestates inside a woman's uterus.
treatment_summary: Prenatal care including regular check-ups, ultrasounds, and nutritional advice.  Lifestyle adjustments such as diet and exercise are generally recommended.

description: A premature atrial contraction (PAC), also known as atrial premature complex (APC) or atrial premature beat (APB), is a common arrhythmia characterized by premature heartbeats originating in the atria.
treatment_summary: Reducing stress and caffeine intake can help. In some cases, medication may be prescribed to control heart rhythm.

description: Primary ovarian insufficiency (POI), also called premature ovarian insufficiency and premature ovarian failure, is the partial or total loss of reproductive and hormonal function of the ovaries before age 40 because of follicular (egg producing area) dysfunction or early loss of eggs.
treatment_summary: Hormone replacement therapy (HRT) can alleviate symptoms and protect bone health. Supportive care addresses emotional and fertility concerns.

description: The amniotic sac, also called the bag of waters or the membranes, is the sac in which the embryo and later fetus develops in amniotes.
treatment_summary: Management depends on gestational age and may involve monitoring for infection, antibiotics, and inducing labor.  The goal is to balance the risks of prematurity with the risks of infection.

description: A premature ventricular contraction (PVC) is a common event where the heartbeat is initiated by Purkinje fibers in the ventricles rather than by the sinoatrial node.
treatment_summary: Reducing caffeine and stress is advisable. Medications may be used to control heart rhythm if PVCs are frequent or symptomatic.

description: Premenstrual syndrome (PMS) is a disruptive set of emotional and physical symptoms that regularly occur in the one to two weeks before the start of each menstrual period.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on symptom management through medication, lifestyle adjustments, and behavioral therapy. Oral contraceptives and antidepressants may be prescribed.

description: Presbycusis (also spelled presbyacusis, from Greek πρέσβυς presbys "old" + ἄκουσις akousis "hearing"), or age-related hearing loss, is the cumulative effect of aging on hearing.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes supportive care, such as hearing aids, to improve hearing ability. Assistive devices and communication strategies can also help.

description: Presbyopia is a physiological insufficiency of optical accommodation associated with the aging of the eye; it results in progressively worsening ability to focus clearly on close objects.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes corrective lenses, such as reading glasses or multifocal lenses. In some cases, refractive surgery may be an option.

related_diseases.disease: retinopathy due to high blood pressure

description: Priapism is a condition in which a penis remains erect for hours in the absence of stimulation or after stimulation has ended.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce the erection and prevent long-term damage. This may involve medication, aspiration, or surgery.

description: Primary immunodeficiencies are disorders in which part of the body's immune system is missing or does not function normally.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies widely depending on the specific type of immunodeficiency. It includes infection prevention and management, and immune-boosting therapies.

description: Insomnia, also known as sleeplessness, is a sleep disorder causing difficulty falling asleep or staying asleep for as long as desired.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes cognitive behavioral therapy, medication, and lifestyle changes to improve sleep habits. Addressing underlying medical or psychological conditions is also important.

description: Kidney diseases, or renal diseases, technically referred to as nephropathies, are pathological conditions affecting the kidneys—the organs that facilitate removal of waste products and toxins from the bloodstream, regulate the body's concentration of electrolytes, maintain the appropriate acid-base balance of plasma, and produce urine as a byproduct of metabolism.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to slow the progression of kidney damage and manage symptoms. This involves medication, dietary changes, and regular monitoring.

description: In hematology, thrombocythemia is a condition of high platelet (thrombocyte) count in the blood.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing the risk of blood clots and managing symptoms. This often involves medication to lower the platelet count and regular monitoring.

description: Complications of pregnancy are health problems that are related to or arise during pregnancy.
treatment_summary: Management depends on the specific complication and may involve medication, surgery, increased monitoring, and supportive care. The goal is to ensure the health of both the mother and the baby.

description: Prostate cancer is the uncontrolled growth of cells in the prostate, a gland in the male reproductive system below the bladder.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include surgery, radiation therapy, hormone therapy, and chemotherapy, depending on the stage and aggressiveness of the cancer. Active surveillance is also used for slow-growing tumors.

description: Prostatitis is an umbrella term for a variety of medical conditions that incorporate bacterial and non-bacterial origin illnesses in the pelvic region.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies depending on the cause, but may include antibiotics, physical therapy, and lifestyle modifications. Pain management and supportive care are also important.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: Protein–energy undernutrition (PEU), once called protein–energy malnutrition (PEM), is a form of malnutrition that is defined as a range of conditions arising from coincident lack of dietary protein and/or energy (calories) in varying proportions.

risk_factors.label: Increased protein requirements (e.g., during growth or illne

treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on increasing protein and calorie intake through diet or supplementation. Underlying medical conditions should also be addressed.

description: Pseudohypoparathyroidism is a rare autosomal dominant genetic condition associated primarily with resistance to the parathyroid hormone.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves managing calcium and phosphate levels with oral supplements. Regular monitoring of hormone levels is essential.

description: Idiopathic intracranial hypertension (IIH), previously known as pseudotumor cerebri and benign intracranial hypertension, is a condition characterized by increased intracranial pressure (pressure around the brain) without a detectable cause.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce intracranial pressure with medication or surgery. Lifestyle changes such as weight loss can also help.

description: Psoriasis is a long-lasting, noncontagious autoimmune disease characterized by patches of abnormal skin.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include topical creams, oral medications, light therapy, and lifestyle modifications. Management focuses on reducing inflammation and skin cell turnover.

description: Psychosexual disorder is a sexual problem that is psychological, rather than physiological in origin.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves psychotherapy to address underlying psychological factors. Medication may be used to manage associated anxiety or depression.

description: In psychopathology, psychosis is the inability to distinguish what is or is not real.
treatment_summary: Antipsychotic medications are the primary treatment, often combined with psychotherapy and social support. Early intervention is important.

description: A pterygium (pl.: pterygia or pterygiums) is any wing-like triangular membrane occurring in the neck, eyes, knees, elbows, ankles or digits.
treatment_summary: Treatment may include lubricating eye drops, topical steroids, or surgical removal if vision is affected. Protection from UV light is important to prevent recurrence.

description: Pulmonary edema (British English: oedema), also known as pulmonary congestion, is excessive fluid accumulation in the tissue or air spaces (usually alveoli) of the lungs.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on removing excess fluid with diuretics and addressing the underlying cause. Oxygen therapy and supportive care may also be needed.

description: Pulmonary embolism (PE) is a blockage of an artery in the lungs by a substance that has moved from elsewhere in the body through the bloodstream (embolism).
treatment_summary: Treatment involves anticoagulant medications to prevent further clots. In severe cases, thrombolytic therapy or surgery may be necessary.

description: Eosinophilia is a condition in which the eosinophil count in the peripheral blood exceeds 0.5×109/L (500/μL).
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on identifying and eliminating the cause, often with corticosteroids to reduce inflammation. Supportive care manages symptoms and prevents complications.

description: Pulmonary fibrosis is typically a condition in which the lungs become scarred over time.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to slow progression and manage symptoms with medications and pulmonary rehabilitation. Lung transplant may be considered in severe cases.

description: Pulmonary hypertension (PH or PHTN) is a condition of increased blood pressure in the arteries of the lungs.
treatment_summary: Medications are used to lower blood pressure in the lungs. In some cases, surgery or lung transplantation may be necessary.

description: Valvular heart disease is any cardiovascular disease process involving one or more of the four valves of the heart (the aortic and mitral valves on the left side of heart and the pulmonic and tricuspid valves on the right side of heart).
treatment_summary: Treatment ranges from monitoring mild cases to medication for symptom management. Surgery may be required to repair or replace the valve in severe cases.

description: Pyelonephritis  is inflammation of the kidney, typically due to a bacterial infection.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antibiotics to eradicate the bacterial infection. Supportive care includes pain management and hydration.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: Pyloric stenosis is a narrowing of the opening from the stomach to the first part of the small intestine (the pylorus).
treatment_summary: Surgical correction, pyloromyotomy, is the primary treatment to widen the pylorus.

description: A pyogenic granuloma or lobular capillary hemangioma is a vascular tumor that occurs on both mucosa and skin, and appears as an overgrowth of tissue due to irritation, physical trauma, or hormonal factors.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes topical or oral antibiotics to kill the bacteria. Proper wound care is essential to prevent recurrence.

description: Raynaud syndrome, also known as Raynaud's phenomenon, is a medical condition in which the spasm of small arteries causes episodes of reduced blood flow to end arterioles.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves lifestyle modifications such as avoiding cold and stress, and medication to improve blood flow. In severe cases, surgery may be considered.

description: Reactive arthritis, previously known as Reiter's syndrome, is a form of inflammatory arthritis that develops in response to an infection in another part of the body (cross-reactivity).
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms with pain relievers, physical therapy, and antibiotics to treat the underlying infection. Corticosteroids or other immunosuppressants may be used in severe cases.

description: A rectal prolapse occurs when walls of the rectum have prolapsed to such a degree that they protrude out of the anus and are visible outside the body.
treatment_summary: Treatment ranges from lifestyle changes and physical therapy to strengthen pelvic floor muscles, to surgery to repair the prolapse.  Stool softeners may alleviate straining.

description: Restless legs syndrome (RLS), also known as Willis–Ekbom disease (WED), is a neurological disorder, usually chronic, that causes an overwhelming urge to move one's legs.

risk_factors.label: Chronic diseases such as kidney failure or diabetes

treatment_summary: Treatment includes medication to control symptoms and lifestyle changes to improve sleep and reduce discomfort. Iron supplements may be recommended for those with iron deficiency.

description: Retinal detachment is a condition where the retina pulls away from the tissue underneath it.
treatment_summary: Retinal detachment is treated with surgery to reattach the retina to the back of the eye.  Prompt treatment is necessary to prevent permanent vision loss.

disease: retinopathy due to high blood pressure
description: Hypertensive retinopathy is damage to the retina and retinal circulation due to high blood pressure (i.e.
treatment_summary: Management focuses on controlling blood pressure through medication and lifestyle changes. Regular monitoring of the retina is essential to detect and manage complications.

related_diseases.disease: central retinal artery or vein occlusion

description: Rhabdomyolysis (shortened as rhabdo) is a condition in which damaged skeletal muscle breaks down rapidly.
treatment_summary: Treatment primarily involves supportive care such as intravenous fluids to prevent kidney damage. Monitoring kidney function and electrolyte levels is crucial.

description: Rheumatic fever (RF) is an inflammatory disease that can involve the heart, joints, skin, and brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antibiotics to eradicate the streptococcal infection and anti-inflammatory medications to reduce inflammation. Supportive care is provided to manage symptoms and prevent complications.

description: Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) is a long-term autoimmune disorder that primarily affects joints.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and prevent joint damage through medication, physical therapy, and lifestyle modifications. Early diagnosis and treatment are essential.

description: Rocky Mountain spotted fever (RMSF) is a bacterial disease spread by ticks.

risk_factors.label: Living in or visiting areas where ticks are prevalent

treatment_summary: Treatment involves antibiotics, particularly doxycycline. Early treatment is crucial to prevent severe complications.

description: Rosacea is a long-term skin condition that typically affects the face.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves topical and oral medications to reduce redness and inflammation, as well as lifestyle changes to avoid triggers. Sun protection is important.

description: The rotator cuff (SITS muscles) is a group of muscles and their tendons that act to stabilize the human shoulder and allow for its extensive range of motion.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes physical therapy to strengthen the muscles, pain medication, and in some cases, surgery to repair the torn rotator cuff.  Avoiding activities that aggravate the injury is important.

description: Salivary gland diseases (SGDs) are multiple and varied in cause.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies depending on the cause, and can include medication for infection or inflammation, surgery to remove blockages or tumors, and supportive care to relieve symptoms. Maintaining hydration is important.

description: Sarcoidosis, also known as Besnier–Boeck–Schaumann disease, is a non-infectious granulomatous disease involving abnormal collections of inflammatory cells that form lumps known as granulomata.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing inflammation and managing symptoms with medications. Supportive care helps manage the disease's impact on daily life.

description: Scabies (, SKAY-beez; also sometimes known as the seven-year itch) is a contagious human skin infestation by the tiny (0.2–0.45 mm) mite Sarcoptes scabiei, variety hominis.
treatment_summary: Scabies is primarily treated with topical medications to kill the mites. Thorough cleaning of bedding and clothing is also necessary to prevent re-infestation.

related_diseases.disease: envenomation from spider or animal bite

description: A scar (or scar tissue) is an area of fibrous tissue that replaces normal skin following an injury.
treatment_summary: Scar treatment varies depending on the scar's severity and location. Options include topical treatments to minimize appearance or surgical procedures for more significant scars.

description: Scarlet fever, also known as scarlatina and scarlatiniform rash, is an infectious disease caused by Streptococcus pyogenes, a Group A streptococcus (GAS).
treatment_summary: Scarlet fever is treated with oral antibiotics to eliminate the streptococcal infection. Treatment helps to prevent complications such as rheumatic fever.

description: Schizophrenia is a mental disorder characterized variously by hallucinations (typically, hearing voices), delusions, disorganized thinking or behavior, and flat or inappropriate affect.
treatment_summary: Schizophrenia is managed with antipsychotic medications to control symptoms and behavioral therapy. Supportive care helps individuals cope with the challenges of the disorder.

description: Sciatica is pain going down the leg from the lower back.
treatment_summary: Sciatica treatment focuses on pain relief and improving mobility through physical therapy. Medications, such as pain relievers, and supportive care are also used to manage symptoms.

description: Scleritis is a serious inflammatory disease that affects the white outer coating of the eye, known as the sclera.
treatment_summary: Scleritis is treated with anti-inflammatory medications, often corticosteroids, to reduce inflammation. In severe cases, immunosuppressants may be necessary.

description: Scleroderma is a group of autoimmune diseases that may result in changes to the skin, blood vessels, muscles, and internal organs.
treatment_summary: Scleroderma treatment aims to manage symptoms and slow disease progression with medications. Physical therapy and supportive care help maintain function and quality of life.

description: Scoliosis (pl.: scolioses) is a medical condition in which the spine has an irregular curve in the coronal plane.
treatment_summary: Scoliosis treatment depends on the severity of the curve, ranging from monitoring and physical therapy to surgery. Bracing is used to prevent curve progression in growing children.

description: Scurvy or scorbutus is a deficiency disease (state of malnutrition) resulting from a lack of vitamin C (ascorbic acid).
treatment_summary: Scurvy is treated with vitamin C supplementation and dietary changes to increase vitamin C intake. Treatment reverses the symptoms of the deficiency.

description: Allergic rhinitis, of which the seasonal type is called hay fever, is a type of inflammation in the nose that occurs when the immune system overreacts to allergens in the air.
treatment_summary: Antihistamines and decongestants can relieve symptoms. Avoiding allergens and using nasal irrigation can also help.

description:
A sebaceous cyst is a term commonly used to refer to either:

Epidermoid cysts (also termed epidermal cysts, infundibular cyst)
Pilar cysts (also termed trichelemmal cysts, isthmus-catagen cysts)
Both of the above types of cysts contain keratin, not sebum, and neither originates from sebaceous glands. Epidermoid cysts originate in the epidermis and pilar cysts originate from hair follicles. Technically speaking, then, they are not sebaceous cysts. "True" sebaceous cysts,  which originate from sebaceous glands and which contain sebum, are relatively rare and are known as steatocystoma simplex or, if multiple, as steatocystoma multiplex.
Medical professionals have suggested that the term "sebaceous cyst" be avoided since it can be misleading. In practice, however, the term is still often used for epidermoid and pilar cysts.
treatment_summary: Small cysts may resolve on their own or with topical treatments. Larger or bothersome cysts can be surgically removed.

description: Seborrhoeic dermatitis (also spelled seborrheic dermatitis in American English) is a long-term skin disorder.
treatment_summary: Topical antifungals and corticosteroids reduce inflammation and yeast. Special shampoos and stress management can help manage the condition.

description: A seborrheic keratosis is a non-cancerous (benign) skin tumour that originates from cells, namely keratinocytes, in the outer layer of the skin called the epidermis.
treatment_summary: Seborrheic keratoses are benign and often require no treatment. Removal options include cryotherapy, curettage, or excision if desired for cosmetic reasons.

description: Sensorineural hearing loss (SNHL) is a type of hearing loss in which the root cause lies in the inner ear, sensory organ (cochlea and associated structures), or the vestibulocochlear nerve (cranial nerve VIII).
treatment_summary: Hearing aids and cochlear implants can help improve hearing. Protecting ears from loud noises is essential for prevention.

description: Sepsis is a potentially life-threatening condition that arises when the body's dysregulated response to infection causes injury to its own tissues and organs.
treatment_summary: Sepsis requires immediate medical attention, including antibiotics and supportive care to manage organ dysfunction. Early diagnosis and treatment are crucial.

description: Acute septic arthritis, infectious arthritis, suppurative arthritis, pyogenic arthritis, osteomyelitis, or joint infection is the invasion of a joint by an infectious agent resulting in joint inflammation.
treatment_summary: Antibiotics are used to treat the infection, and the joint may need to be drained surgically. Early treatment is essential to prevent joint damage.

description: A zoster vaccine is a vaccine that reduces the incidence of herpes zoster (a.k.a.
treatment_summary: Antiviral medications can reduce the severity and duration of shingles. Pain relievers and supportive care can help manage symptoms.

description: Sialadenitis (sialoadenitis) is inflammation of salivary glands, usually the major ones, the most common being the parotid gland, followed by submandibular and sublingual glands.
treatment_summary: Antibiotics are used to treat bacterial infections, and supportive care includes hydration and warm compresses. Surgery may be needed to remove salivary duct stones.

description: Sinus node dysfunction (SND), also known as sick sinus syndrome (SSS), is a group of abnormal heart rhythms (arrhythmias) usually caused by a malfunction of the sinus node, the heart's primary pacemaker.
treatment_summary: Medications can manage symptoms, and a pacemaker may be implanted to regulate the heart rhythm. Regular monitoring is important.

description: Sickle cell disease (SCD), also simply called sickle cell, is a group of inherited haemoglobin-related blood disorders.
treatment_summary: Treatments focus on managing symptoms and preventing complications. Blood transfusions and bone marrow transplants can provide more definitive treatment.

description: Sickle cell disease (SCD), also simply called sickle cell, is a group of inherited haemoglobin-related blood disorders.
treatment_summary: Pain management and supportive care are crucial during a sickle cell crisis. Hydration and oxygen therapy are often required.

description: Sinus bradycardia is a sinus rhythm with a reduced rate of electrical discharge from the sinoatrial node, resulting in a bradycardia, a heart rate that is lower than the normal range (60–100 beats per minute for adult humans).
treatment_summary: Treatment is only required if the slow heart rate causes symptoms. Medications or a pacemaker may be needed in some cases.

description: Sjögren's disease (SjD), previously known as Sjögren syndrome or Sjögren's syndrome (SjS, SS), is a long-term autoimmune disease that primarily affects the body's exocrine glands, particularly the lacrimal and salivary glands.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on relieving symptoms like dry eyes and dry mouth. Immunosuppressant medications may be used in severe cases.

related_diseases.disease: central retinal artery or vein occlusion
retinopathy due to high blood pressure

description: Skin cancers are cancers that arise from the skin.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the type and stage of skin cancer. Options include surgical removal, radiation therapy, and chemotherapy.

description: A skin condition, also known as a cutaneous condition, is any medical condition that affects the integumentary system—the organ system that encloses the body and includes skin, nails, and related muscle and glands.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies widely based on the specific skin disorder. Options range from topical creams to oral medications and lifestyle adjustments.

description: Pigmentation disorders are disturbances of human skin color.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to even out skin tone by reducing or increasing pigment. Options include topical creams, laser therapy, and sun protection.

description: A skin tag, or acrochordon (pl.: acrochorda), is a small benign tumor that forms primarily in areas where the skin forms creases (or rubs together), such as the neck, armpit and groin.
treatment_summary: Skin tags are typically harmless and don't require treatment. They can be easily removed by surgical excision, cryotherapy, or other methods.

description: Tobacco products, especially when smoked or used orally, have serious negative effects on human health.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes behavioral therapy, medications to reduce cravings, and support groups. Lifestyle changes and avoiding triggers are also helpful.

description: Social anxiety disorder (SAD), also known as social phobia, is an anxiety disorder characterized by sentiments of fear and anxiety in social situations, causing considerable distress and impairing ability to function in at least some aspects of daily life.
treatment_summary: Treatment usually involves cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) and/or medication. Lifestyle changes, like practicing relaxation techniques, may also help.

description: A soft-tissue sarcoma (STS) is a malignant tumor, a type of cancer, that develops in soft tissue.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves surgery to remove the tumor. Radiation and chemotherapy may also be used, depending on the stage and type of sarcoma.

description: Somatic symptom disorder (SSD) is a mental health condition characterised by an excessive focus on physical symptoms—such as pain or shortness of breath—that cause significant distress or impairment.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and improving quality of life through therapy and medication. Cognitive behavioral therapy can help manage thoughts and behaviors related to physical symptoms.

description: Spermatocele is a fluid-filled cyst that develops in the epididymis.
treatment_summary: Often, no treatment is needed as spermatoceles are usually harmless. If the spermatocele causes pain or discomfort, surgical removal may be considered.

description: Spherocytosis is the presence of spherocytes in the blood, i.e.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include medication to manage anemia and splenectomy to reduce red blood cell destruction. Supportive care, such as folic acid supplementation, is also important.

description: Spina bifida (SB; ; Latin for 'split spine') is a birth defect in which there is incomplete closing of the spine and the membranes around the spinal cord during early development in pregnancy.

risk_factors.label: Family history of neural tube defects

treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery to close the opening in the spine shortly after birth. Ongoing supportive care and physical therapy are crucial for managing complications.

description: Spinal stenosis is an abnormal narrowing of the spinal canal or neural foramen that results in pressure on the spinal cord or nerve roots.
treatment_summary: Treatment options range from physical therapy and pain medication to surgery. Surgery aims to relieve pressure on the spinal cord or nerves.

description: Spinocerebellar ataxia (SCA) is a progressive, degenerative, genetic disease with multiple types, each of which could be considered a neurological condition in its own right.
treatment_summary: There is no cure for spinocerebellar ataxia; treatment focuses on managing symptoms and improving quality of life. Physical therapy and assistive devices can help with mobility and balance.

description: Spondylitis is an inflammation of the vertebrae.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing inflammation and pain. Medications, physical therapy, and lifestyle adjustments are commonly used.

description: Spondylolisthesis refers to a condition in which one spinal vertebra slips out of place compared to another.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies depending on the severity of the slip and may include physical therapy, pain medication, or surgery. Lifestyle modifications, such as weight management and avoiding strenuous activities, can also help.

description: Spondylosis is the degeneration of the vertebral column from any cause.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to relieve pain and improve function through physical therapy, pain medication, and lifestyle changes. In some cases, surgery may be necessary.

description: Miscarriage, also known in medical terms as a spontaneous abortion, is an end to pregnancy resulting in the loss and expulsion of an embryo or fetus from the womb before it can survive independently.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing the physical and emotional aspects of pregnancy loss. Surgical procedures such as dilation and curettage (D&C) may be necessary.

description: Sporotrichosis, also known as rose handler's disease, is a fungal infection that may be localised to skin, lungs, bone and joint, or become systemic.
treatment_summary: Antifungal medications are the primary treatment. The duration of treatment depends on the severity and location of the infection.

description: A sprain is a soft tissue injury of the ligaments within a joint, often caused by a sudden movement abruptly forcing the joint to exceed its functional range of motion.
treatment_summary: Initial treatment involves rest, ice, compression, and elevation (RICE). Physical therapy and pain relievers may also be used.

description: Nasolacrimal duct obstruction is the obstruction of the nasolacrimal ducts (better known as the tear ducts) and may be either congenital or acquired.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include massage, warm compresses, antibiotics and surgery. Surgery may be necessary to open the blocked duct.

description: Stomach cancer, also known as gastric cancer, is a malignant tumor of the stomach.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery to remove the tumor, chemotherapy, and radiation therapy. Palliative care focuses on managing symptoms and improving quality of life.

description: Streptococcal pharyngitis, also known as streptococcal sore  throat (strep throat), is pharyngitis (an infection of the pharynx, the back of the throat) caused by Streptococcus pyogenes, a gram-positive, group A streptococcus.
treatment_summary: Antibiotics are used to treat strep throat and prevent complications. Pain relievers can help manage symptoms.

description: Stress incontinence, also known as stress urinary incontinence (SUI) or effort incontinence is a form of urinary incontinence.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include pelvic floor exercises, lifestyle changes such as weight loss, and medications. Surgery is sometimes recommended.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: A benign esophageal stricture, or peptic stricture, is a narrowing or tightening of the esophagus that causes swallowing difficulties.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves dilating the esophagus and managing underlying causes like acid reflux. Medications to reduce stomach acid may be prescribed.

description: Stroke is a medical condition in which poor blood flow to a part of the brain causes cell death.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on restoring blood flow to the brain and preventing complications. Physical therapy helps regain lost function.

description: A stye, also known as a hordeolum, is a bacterial infection of an oil gland in the eyelid.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes warm compresses and topical antibiotics. In rare cases, the stye may need to be lanced and drained.

description: Subacute thyroiditis refers to a temporal classification of the different forms of thyroiditis based on onset of symptoms.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing pain and inflammation with medications such as NSAIDs or corticosteroids. Monitoring thyroid hormone levels is also important.

description: Subarachnoid hemorrhage (SAH) is bleeding into the subarachnoid space—the area between the arachnoid membrane and the pia mater surrounding the brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes surgical intervention to repair the source of bleeding and medications to manage blood pressure and prevent complications. Supportive care is crucial.

description: Subconjunctival bleeding, also known as subconjunctival hemorrhage or subconjunctival haemorrhage, is bleeding from a small blood vessel over the whites of the eye.
treatment_summary: Usually, no treatment is required as the condition resolves on its own within a couple of weeks. Monitoring for complications may be recommended.

related_diseases.disease: retinopathy due to high blood pressure
central retinal artery or vein occlusion

description: A subdural hematoma (SDH) is a type of bleeding in which a collection of blood—usually but not always associated with a traumatic brain injury—gathers between the inner layer of the dura mater and the arachnoid mater of the meninges surrounding the brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment ranges from monitoring to surgical intervention to remove the blood clot. Medications may be used to manage symptoms and prevent further complications.

description: Substance-related disorders are a class of disorders listed in the DSM-5 that are "related to the taking of a drug of abuse (including alcohol)".
treatment_summary: Treatment involves behavioral therapy, medication to manage withdrawal and cravings, and supportive care. Addressing underlying mental health issues is also critical.

disease: syndrome of inappropriate secretion of adh (siadh)
description: Syndrome of inappropriate antidiuretic hormone secretion (SIADH), also known as the syndrome of inappropriate antidiuresis (SIAD), is characterized by a physiologically inappropriate release of antidiuretic hormone (ADH) either from the posterior pituitary gland, or an ectopic non-pituitary source, such as an ADH-secreting tumor in the lung.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on addressing the underlying cause and restricting fluid intake. Medications may be used to manage the electrolyte imbalance.

description: Syphilis () is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pallidum.
treatment_summary: Syphilis is treated with antibiotics, usually penicillin. Early treatment can prevent serious complications.

description: Syringomyelia is a generic term referring to a disorder in which a cyst or cavity forms within the spinal cord.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include surgery to relieve pressure on the spinal cord, physical therapy to improve function, and medication to manage pain. Monitoring is often required.

description: Lupus, formally called systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE), is an autoimmune disease in which the body's immune system mistakenly attacks healthy tissue in many parts of the body.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and preventing organ damage with medications like corticosteroids and immunosuppressants. Lifestyle changes, such as sun protection, are also important.

description: Teething is the process by which an infant's first teeth (the deciduous teeth, often called "baby teeth" or "milk teeth") appear by emerging through the gums, typically arriving in pairs.
treatment_summary: Treatment is supportive and focuses on relieving discomfort with teething rings or gentle gum massage. Pain relievers may be considered in some cases.

disease: temporary or benign blood in urine
description: Benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), also called prostate enlargement, is a noncancerous increase in size of the prostate gland.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes medication to relax the prostate or shrink it, surgery to remove or destroy prostate tissue, and lifestyle changes. Monitoring is also important to track the progression.

description: Temporomandibular joint dysfunction (TMD, TMJD) is an umbrella term for a spectrum of disorders relating to pain and dysfunction of the muscles of mastication (the muscles that move the jaw) and the temporomandibular joints (the joints which connect the mandible to the skull).
treatment_summary: Treatment options include pain medications, physical therapy to improve joint mobility, behavioral therapy to address teeth grinding, and lifestyle changes. In some cases, surgery is required.

description: Tendinopathy is a type of tendon disorder that results in pain, swelling, and impaired function.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes pain relief medication (oral or topical), physical therapy to strengthen the affected tendon, and lifestyle changes to avoid aggravating activities. Rest and ice are also important components.

description: Tension headache, stress headache, or tension-type headache (TTH), is the most common type of primary headache.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes over-the-counter pain relievers, behavioral therapy to manage stress, and lifestyle changes like improving posture and sleep habits. Relaxation techniques can also be helpful.

description: Testicular cancer is cancer that develops in the testicles, a part of the male reproductive system.
treatment_summary: Treatment commonly involves surgery to remove the affected testicle, followed by chemotherapy or radiation therapy if the cancer has spread.  Early detection is key for successful treatment.

description: A testicle, also called testis (pl.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the specific disorder but may include antibiotics for infections, surgery for structural problems, or supportive care for pain management. Monitoring is generally needed.

description: Testicular torsion occurs when the spermatic cord (from which the testicle is suspended) twists, cutting off the blood supply to the testicle.
treatment_summary: Testicular torsion requires immediate surgery to untwist the spermatic cord and restore blood flow to the testicle. If not treated quickly, the testicle can be damaged or lost.

description: Thalassemias are a group of inherited blood disorders that manifest as the production of reduced hemoglobin.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies depending on the severity and type of thalassemia, ranging from blood transfusions and iron chelation therapy to bone marrow transplantation. Supportive care focuses on managing complications.

description: A thoracic aortic aneurysm is an aortic aneurysm that presents primarily in the thorax.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves monitoring the aneurysm's size, medication to control blood pressure, lifestyle changes such as quitting smoking, and surgery to repair or replace the affected section of the aorta.  The goal is to prevent rupture.

description: Thoracic outlet syndrome (TOS) is a condition in which there is compression of the nerves, arteries, or veins in the superior thoracic aperture, the passageway from the lower neck to the armpit, also known as the thoracic outlet.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes physical therapy to improve posture and range of motion, pain medication, and, in some cases, surgery to relieve pressure on the nerves and blood vessels. Lifestyle changes like avoiding repetitive movements are also recommended.

description: Ectopic pregnancy is a complication of pregnancy in which the embryo attaches outside the uterus.
treatment_summary: Treatment may involve medication to terminate the pregnancy or surgery to remove the ectopic pregnancy. Careful monitoring is essential to manage potential complications.

description: In hematology, thrombocytopenia is a condition characterized by abnormally low levels of platelets (also known as thrombocytes) in the blood.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the cause and severity, and may include medications to increase platelet count or supportive care such as transfusions. Addressing the underlying cause is also crucial.

description: Thrombophlebitis is a phlebitis (inflammation of a vein) related to a thrombus (blood clot).
treatment_summary: Treatment usually involves medications to prevent further clot formation and reduce pain. Supportive care includes elevation of the affected limb and compression therapy.

description: Thyroid cancer is cancer that develops from the tissues of the thyroid gland.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves surgical removal of the thyroid gland, followed by thyroid hormone replacement therapy. Radioactive iodine therapy may also be used to destroy any remaining cancer cells.

description: Thyroid disease is a medical condition that affects the structure and/or function of the thyroid gland.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies depending on the specific thyroid disorder, and may include medication to regulate hormone levels or surgery to remove part or all of the thyroid gland. Regular monitoring is essential.

related_diseases.disease: foreign body in the gastrointestinal tract

description: Thyroid nodules are nodules (raised areas of tissue or fluid) which commonly arise within an otherwise normal thyroid gland.
treatment_summary: Most thyroid nodules are benign and require only monitoring. If the nodule is cancerous or causing symptoms, treatment options include surgery or thyroid hormone suppression with medication.

description: Tic disorders are defined in the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM) based on type (motor or phonic) and duration of tics (sudden, rapid, nonrhythmic movements).
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing tics and any associated conditions. Behavioral therapy and medication can help reduce the frequency and severity of tics.

description: Tietze syndrome is a benign inflammation of one or more of the costal cartilages.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on pain relief and reducing inflammation. Pain relievers and anti-inflammatory medications are commonly used, along with rest and heat or ice application.
