description: Ménière's disease (MD) is a disease of the inner ear characterized by potentially severe and incapacitating episodes of vertigo, tinnitus, hearing loss, and a feeling of fullness in the ear.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing the symptoms and improving quality of life since the cause is unknown. Options include tinnitus retraining therapy and sound masking devices.

description: Palatine tonsils, commonly called the tonsils and occasionally called the faucial tonsils, are tonsils located on the left and right sides at the back of the throat in humans and other mammals, which can often be seen as flesh-colored, pinkish lumps.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the severity of symptoms. Options range from antibiotics for infections to surgical removal of the tonsils (tonsillectomy) in severe cases.

description: Tonsillitis is inflammation of the tonsils in the upper part of the throat.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves managing symptoms with pain relievers and fluids. Antibiotics may be needed if bacterial; surgery is reserved for recurrent cases.

description: A dental abscess is a localized collection of pus associated with a tooth.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes draining the abscess and antibiotics to treat infection. Root canal or tooth extraction may be necessary.

description: Tooth pathology is any condition of the teeth that can be congenital or acquired.
treatment_summary: Treatments vary widely based on the specific tooth disorder. Options include fillings, root canals, orthodontics, or extractions.

description: Torticollis, also known as wry neck, is an extremely painful, dystonic condition defined by an abnormal, asymmetrical head or neck position, which may be due to a variety of causes.
treatment_summary: Treatment may include physical therapy to stretch muscles. Medications may be used to relieve pain and muscle spasms.

description: Tourette syndrome (TS), or simply Tourette's, is a motor disorder that begins in childhood or adolescence.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing tics with behavioral therapies and medication. Therapy can help individuals learn to manage tics.

description: Toxic multinodular goiter (TMNG), also known as multinodular toxic goiter (MNTG), is an active multinodular goiter associated with hyperthyroidism.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to reduce thyroid hormone production, often with medication. Surgery or radioactive iodine therapy may be needed.

description: Toxoplasmosis is a parasitic disease caused by Toxoplasma gondii, an apicomplexan.
treatment_summary: Treatment is usually only necessary for pregnant women or those with weakened immune systems. Medications target the parasite.

description: Tracheitis is an inflammation of the trachea.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antibiotics for bacterial infections. Supportive care includes fluids and monitoring breathing.

description: A transient ischemic attack (TIA), commonly known as mini-stroke, is temporary, or transient, stroke with noticeable symptoms that end within 24 hours.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on preventing future strokes by managing risk factors. Medications include antiplatelet agents and lifestyle changes such as diet and exercise are recommended.

description: Trichiasis ( trik-ee-AY-sis,  tri-KEYE-ə-sis) is a medical term for abnormally positioned eyelashes that grow back toward the eye, touching the cornea or conjunctiva.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves removing or redirecting the misdirected eyelashes. This can be done through various methods, including electrolysis or surgery.

description: Trichinosis, also known as trichinellosis, is a parasitic disease caused by roundworms of the Trichinella genus.
treatment_summary: Medication is given to kill the parasite and reduce inflammation. Supportive care focuses on managing symptoms like pain and fever.

description: Trichomonas vaginalis is an anaerobic, flagellated protozoan parasite and the causative agent of a sexually transmitted disease called trichomoniasis.
treatment_summary: Oral antibiotics are prescribed to treat the infection. Sexual partners should also be treated to prevent reinfection.

description: The tricuspid valve, or right atrioventricular valve, is on the right dorsal side of the mammalian heart, at the superior portion of the right ventricle.
treatment_summary: Medications can manage symptoms; severe cases may need surgical valve repair or replacement. Regular monitoring is necessary to assess valve function.

description: Trigeminal neuralgia (TN or TGN), also called Fothergill disease, tic douloureux, or trifacial neuralgia, is a long-term pain disorder that affects the trigeminal nerve, the nerve responsible for sensation in the face and motor functions such as biting and chewing.
treatment_summary: Medications help manage the nerve pain. Surgical options are available for more severe cases that do not respond to medication.

description: Trigger finger, also known as stenosing tenosynovitis, is  a disorder characterized by catching or locking of the involved finger in full or near full flexion, typically when the hand is closed with force.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves pain relief, physical therapy, and sometimes surgery. Oral medications such as NSAIDs and corticosteroid injections into the tendon sheath can alleviate pain.

description: Tuberculosis (TB), also known colloquially as the "white death", or historically as consumption, is a contagious disease usually caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis (MTB) bacteria.
treatment_summary: Tuberculosis is treated with a course of antibiotics. Treatment usually involves taking several drugs for 6 to 9 months.

description: Tuberous sclerosis complex (TSC) is a rare, multi-system genetic disorder that causes benign tumors to grow in various organs, including the brain, kidneys, heart, liver, eyes, lungs, and skin.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on managing symptoms and complications, as there is no cure. Medications can help control seizures and behavioral issues, and surgery may be needed for tumors.

description: Turner syndrome (TS), commonly known as 45,X, or 45,X0, is a chromosomal disorder in which cells of females have only one X chromosome instead of two, or are partially missing an X chromosome (sex chromosome monosomy) leading to the complete or partial deletion of the pseudoautosomal regions (PAR1, PAR2) in the affected X chromosome.

risk_factors.label: Random error during formation of egg or sperm

treatment_summary: Growth hormone therapy and estrogen replacement therapy are common treatments. Regular monitoring for associated health problems is essential.

description: Typhoid fever, also known as typhoid, is a disease caused by Salmonella enterica serotype Typhi bacteria, also called Salmonella Typhi.
treatment_summary: Antibiotics are used to treat the bacterial infection. Supportive care includes rehydration and managing symptoms.

description: Ulcerative colitis (UC) is one of the two types of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), with the other type being Crohn's disease.
treatment_summary: Medications reduce inflammation and control symptoms. Surgery to remove the colon may be necessary in severe cases and dietary changes can help manage symptoms.

description: A urethral stricture is a narrowing of the urethra, the tube connected to the bladder that allows urination.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery to widen the urethra. Medication can help manage symptoms and prevent infection.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: A urethral stricture is a narrowing of the urethra, the tube connected to the bladder that allows urination.
treatment_summary: Treatment often involves surgery to widen the urethra. Medication can help manage symptoms and prevent infection.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: Posterior urethral valve (PUV) disorder is an obstructive developmental anomaly in the urethra and genitourinary system of male newborns.
treatment_summary: Treatment primarily involves surgical intervention to correct the valve obstruction.

description: Urethritis is the inflammation of the urethra.
treatment_summary: Urethritis is typically treated with antibiotics to eliminate the underlying infection.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: Urinary incontinence (UI), also known as involuntary urination, is any uncontrolled leakage of urine.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves behavioral therapies and medications to control bladder function. Lifestyle changes may reduce symptoms.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: A urinary tract infection (UTI) is an infection that affects a part of the urinary tract, which includes the bladder, urethra and the kidney.
treatment_summary: UTIs are usually treated with antibiotics. Supportive care can help manage symptoms.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: Urinary tract obstruction is a urologic disease consisting of a decrease in the free passage of urine through one or both ureters and/or the urethra.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the cause and severity, but surgery is often needed to remove the obstruction. Medications can help manage symptoms.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: Uterine atony is the failure of the uterus to contract adequately following delivery.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on stopping the bleeding and contracting the uterus. Medications and supportive measures are used.

description: Uterine cancer, also known as womb cancer, includes two types of cancer that develop from the tissues of the uterus.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes surgery to remove the uterus, followed by radiation or chemotherapy. Palliative care can help manage symptoms.

description: Uterine fibroids, also known as uterine leiomyomas, fibromyoma or fibroids, are benign smooth muscle tumors of the uterus, part of the female reproductive system.
treatment_summary: Treatment ranges from medications to manage symptoms, to surgical removal. Monitoring may be sufficient for small or asymptomatic fibroids.

description: Uveitis () is inflammation of the uvea, the pigmented layer of the eye between the inner retina and the outer fibrous layer composed of the sclera and cornea.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing inflammation and pain. Medications like corticosteroids and, in some cases, surgery may be necessary to prevent vision loss.

related_diseases.disease: central retinal artery or vein occlusion
retinopathy due to high blood pressure

description: The VACTERL association (also VATER association, and less accurately VACTERL syndrome) refers to a recognized group of birth defects which tend to co-occur (see below).
treatment_summary: Treatment is symptomatic and supportive, addressing each defect individually. Surgery is often required to correct the various congenital anomalies.

description: Vaginal cysts are uncommon benign cysts that develop in the vaginal wall.
treatment_summary: Small, asymptomatic cysts may only require monitoring. Larger, symptomatic cysts may require surgical removal.

description: Vaginal yeast infection, also known as candidal vulvovaginitis and vaginal thrush, is excessive growth of yeast in the vagina that results in irritation.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves antifungal medications. These medications can be administered topically or orally to eradicate the yeast overgrowth.

description: Vaginismus is a condition in which involuntary muscle spasm interferes with vaginal intercourse or other penetration of the vagina.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes therapy to address psychological factors and physical therapy. This can help to reduce muscle spasms and discomfort.

description: Vaginitis, also known as vulvovaginitis, is inflammation of the vagina and vulva.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the cause, involving antibiotics for bacterial infections or antifungals for yeast infections. Avoiding irritants is also important.

description: Coccidioidomycosis (, kok-SID-ee-oy-doh-my-KOH-sis) is a mammalian fungal disease caused by Coccidioides immitis or Coccidioides posadasii.

risk_factors.label: Living in or traveling to endemic areas

treatment_summary: Mild cases often resolve on their own with supportive care. More severe infections may require antifungal medications.

description: A varicocele is an abnormal enlargement of the pampiniform venous plexus in the scrotum.
treatment_summary: Treatment may involve monitoring for mild cases or surgery to correct the enlarged veins. Surgery is recommended when there is pain, infertility, or testicular atrophy.

description: Varicose veins, also known as varicoses, are a medical condition in which superficial veins become enlarged and twisted.
treatment_summary: Treatment options range from lifestyle changes like compression stockings and exercise to surgical procedures. Surgery aims to remove or close off the affected veins.

description: Vasculitis is a group of disorders that destroy blood vessels by inflammation.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing inflammation with medications like corticosteroids and immunosuppressants. Supportive care helps manage symptoms and prevent complications.

description: Chronic venous insufficiency (CVI) is a medical condition characterized by blood pooling in the veins, leading to increased pressure and strain on the vein walls.
treatment_summary: Treatment aims to improve blood flow and reduce symptoms. Options include lifestyle changes, compression therapy, medication, and in severe cases, surgical intervention.

description: Vertebrobasilar insufficiency (VBI) describes a temporary set of symptoms due to decreased blood flow (ischemia) in the posterior circulation of the brain.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on improving blood flow to the brain. This may involve medications to manage blood pressure or cholesterol, lifestyle modifications, or surgical procedures to open blocked arteries.

description: Vesicoureteral reflux (VUR), also known as vesicoureteric reflux, is a condition in which urine flows retrograde, or backward, from the bladder into one or both ureters and then to the renal calyx or kidneys.
treatment_summary: Treatment options vary depending on the severity and may include antibiotics to prevent infections, monitoring, or surgery to correct the anatomical defect.

related_diseases.disease: temporary or benign blood in urine

description: An exanthem is a widespread rash eruption occurring on the outside of the body, usually presents in children, the rash is typically associated with constitutional symptoms including fever and fatigue.
treatment_summary: Treatment mainly focuses on providing supportive care. Rest and hydration are key, and medications may be given to reduce fever and itching.

description: Viral hepatitis is liver inflammation due to a viral infection.
treatment_summary: Treatment depends on the type of hepatitis and can range from supportive care and antiviral medications to liver transplantation in severe cases. Vaccination is available for some types of viral hepatitis.

description: Warts are non-cancerous viral growths usually occurring on the hands and feet but which can also affect other locations, such as the genitals or face.
treatment_summary: Warts can often resolve on their own, but treatment options include topical medications to destroy the wart tissue or surgical removal for persistent cases.

description: Vitamin A deficiency (VAD) or hypovitaminosis A is a lack of vitamin A in blood and tissues.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves oral vitamin A supplementation and addressing underlying malabsorption if present. Dietary changes to include vitamin A-rich foods are also important.

description: Vitamin B12 deficiency, also known as cobalamin deficiency, is the medical condition in which the blood and tissue have a lower than normal level of vitamin B12.
treatment_summary: Vitamin B12 deficiency is treated with oral or injectable vitamin B12 supplements. Dietary modifications and management of underlying malabsorption disorders are also key.

description: Vitamin D deficiency or hypovitaminosis D is a vitamin D level that is below normal.
treatment_summary: Treatment consists of vitamin D supplementation, often through oral medications. Increasing sun exposure and consuming vitamin D-rich foods are also recommended.

description: The vitreous body (vitreous meaning "glass-like"; from Latin  vitreus 'glassy', from  vitrum 'glass' and  -eus) is the clear gel that fills the space between the lens and the retina of the eyeball (the vitreous chamber) in humans and other vertebrates.
treatment_summary: Vitreous degeneration is usually monitored unless complications arise, such as retinal detachment. In those cases, surgery might be necessary.

description: Vitreous hemorrhage is the extravasation, or leakage, of blood into the areas in and around the vitreous humor of the eye.
treatment_summary: Treatment options include observation, laser photocoagulation, or vitrectomy. The approach depends on the severity and underlying cause.

related_diseases.disease: central retinal artery or vein occlusion

description: Vocal cord nodules are bilaterally symmetrical benign white masses (nodules)  that form at the midpoint of the vocal folds.
treatment_summary: Treatment includes voice therapy to correct vocal habits, and sometimes surgical removal of the polyp. Lifestyle changes such as quitting smoking can also help.

description: A volvulus is a bowel obstruction resulting from a loop of intestine twisting around itself and its supporting mesentery.
treatment_summary: Volvulus typically requires prompt surgical intervention to untwist the bowel and prevent tissue death. Supportive care includes fluid resuscitation and electrolyte management.

related_diseases.disease: foreign body in the gastrointestinal tract

description: Von Willebrand disease (VWD) is a type of blood-clotting disorder.
treatment_summary: Treatment may involve medications to increase VWF levels or prevent clot breakdown, and measures to control bleeding.  Supportive care addresses specific bleeding episodes.

description: Vulvar cancer is a cancer of the vulva, the outer portion of the female genitals.
treatment_summary: Treatment typically involves surgical removal of the tumor, radiation therapy, and possibly chemotherapy. Topical medications can address some symptoms.

description: In mammals, the vulva (pl.: vulvas or vulvae) comprises mostly external, visible structures of the female genitalia leading into the interior of the female reproductive tract.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on symptom relief using topical medications such as corticosteroids or antifungal creams. Avoiding irritants and allergens is also important.

description: Vulvodynia is a chronic pain condition that affects the vulvar area and occurs without an identifiable cause.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves pain management with topical medications, physical therapy to address pelvic floor dysfunction, and behavioral therapy. Lifestyle changes such as avoiding irritants can also help.

description: Wernicke–Korsakoff syndrome (WKS), colloquially referred to as wet brain syndrome, is the combined presence of Wernicke encephalopathy (WE) and Korsakoff syndrome.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves thiamine supplementation, supportive care, and management of alcohol withdrawal if present.  Long-term care may include behavioral therapy and dietary changes.

description: White blood cells (scientific name leukocytes), also called immune cells or immunocytes, are cells of the immune system that are involved in protecting the body against both infectious disease and foreign entities.
treatment_summary: Treatment varies greatly depending on the specific disease and may include medications, bone marrow transplant, or supportive care. The goal is to manage symptoms and prevent complications.

description: Whooping cough ( or ), also known as pertussis or the 100-day cough, is a highly contagious, vaccine-preventable bacterial disease.
treatment_summary: Treatment involves antibiotics to reduce the severity and spread of infection, and supportive care to manage symptoms such as coughing. Vaccination is the best prevention.

description: Wilson's disease (also called hepatolenticular degeneration) is a genetic disorder characterized by the excess build-up of copper in the body.
treatment_summary: Treatment focuses on reducing copper levels in the body using chelating agents.  Long-term monitoring is required to manage the condition and prevent complications.

description: Candidiasis is a fungal infection due to any species of yeasts in the genus Candida or those that were formerly classified in genus Candida but are now classified in the genus Candidozyma.
treatment_summary: Antifungal medications, either topical or oral, are commonly used to treat yeast infections. The choice of medication depends on the location and severity of the infection.

description: A Zenker's diverticulum, also pharyngeal pouch, is a diverticulum of the mucosa of the human pharynx, just above the cricopharyngeal muscle (i.e.
treatment_summary: The primary treatment for Zenker's diverticulum is surgical removal or endoscopic stapling of the pouch. This relieves the symptoms of dysphagia and regurgitation.
